method: 23 specialist review agents, full-coverage reads, evidence-cited
note: Full per-finding evidence, impact, root cause, recommendation and acceptance criteria for every finding referenced by section D of the report. Raw (pre-deduplication) set; P0/P1 consolidate into the 17 conflicts of report section E.

findings.title: Business-doc count is stated inconsistently across governance docs and matches no actual count
findings.evidenceA: AGENTS.md:38 — "127 Markdown contracts organized by business object / aggregate"
findings.evidenceB: docs/wbs/memox-v6-development-wbs.md:569 — "because 126 business docs contain many branch-heavy contracts"; contradicted by the same document's own §3.1 (line 31): "use docs/business/README.md as the generated/current denominator; do not freeze a hand-maintained file count here."
findings.description: Two catalog/portal documents each assert a different fixed count of business documents (127 vs 126). Neither matches the actual filesystem: `find docs/business -name '*.md'` returns 135 files total, or 116 excluding the 19 README.md index files. The WBS document explicitly instructs elsewhere (§3.1) not to freeze such a hand-maintained count, then does so anyway in §10 — a self-contradiction within one document, compounded by disagreeing with AGENTS.md's number.
findings.impact: A reader/agent citing either number as ground truth for coverage/completeness reporting will be wrong by 8-19 files; the self-contradiction in the WBS undermines confidence that other WBS baseline numbers (e.g. '27 canonical screens and 211 canonical states', '246 guard rules') are kept in sync with reality.
findings.recommendation: Remove the hardcoded counts from AGENTS.md and WBS §10, or replace them with a generated/verified figure sourced the same way §3.1 already prescribes (derive from docs/business/README.md / a script), and keep the two documents in sync.

findings.title: Stale 'deck-detail' path references survive in two design-kit artifacts after the feature was renamed/merged into library
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/audit/_dts_list.txt:15-19 — lists `ui_kits/memox-app/_features/deck-detail/components/DeckHeader.d.ts`, `DeckMenu.d.ts`, `DeleteConfirmDialog.d.ts`, `FlashcardRow.d.ts`, `SubDeckCard.d.ts`
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/_adherence.oxlintrc.json:27-28 — still whitelists `ui_kits/memox-app/_features/deck-detail/**` and `.../deck-detail/components/**`; but `ls ui_kits/memox-app/_features/` (30 current feature dirs) contains no `deck-detail` directory, and CHANGELOG.md's 'Changed — unified Deck model (2026-07-17)' entry documents the deck-detail feature being folded into `library`/`subdeck-list`.
findings.description: This is documentation/config drift, not a broken markdown link, so it is invisible to the automated 341-file link checker (these are a plain .txt path list and a JSON lint-config allowlist, not markdown links). The referenced directory genuinely no longer exists on disk.
findings.impact: Low direct impact since these paths are inert (a config allowlist entry that matches nothing, and a generated-looking list file that is not linked from any prose page), but anyone trusting `_dts_list.txt` or the oxlint allowlist as current will be misled about the actual `_features/` layout, and the config drift indicates the rename documented in CHANGELOG.md was not fully propagated.
findings.recommendation: Regenerate/prune `_dts_list.txt` and remove the dead `deck-detail` entries from `_adherence.oxlintrc.json` as part of closing the CHANGELOG's 'unified Deck model' migration, or explicitly note in CHANGELOG.md that these two files are known-stale historical artifacts.

findings.title: Historical UI/UX audit snapshot is not reachable from the design kit's documented Index/manifest
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/readme.md:71-85 ('Index / manifest' section) — enumerates `styles.css`, `tokens/`, `components.css`, `components/`, `guidelines/`, `ui_kits/memox-app/`, `fonts/`, `SKILL.md`; does not mention `audit/`.
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/audit/UI-UX Audit.html:1-45 — a 552-line historical point-in-time audit ('22 màn hình · 117 states ... 06/07/2026') that self-labels with a clear 'historical snapshot' warning banner but is referenced nowhere in prose (readme.md, CHANGELOG.md, SCOPE.md); the only repo-wide hit for `audit/UI-UX` is a machine path inside `_ds_manifest.json`.
findings.description: The file correctly self-identifies as a stale historical snapshot (mitigating the risk of it being mistaken for current), but it is an orphan from the human-navigable documentation portal described in readme.md's own 'Index / manifest' section, which claims to be the map of the kit's contents.
findings.impact: Low — the file's own banner prevents most misreadings, but a reviewer following only the documented Index/manifest path would never discover this file exists, and the readme.md index is technically incomplete (it omits the `audit/` folder entirely).
findings.recommendation: Either add a one-line pointer to `audit/UI-UX Audit.html` (marked historical) in readme.md's Index/manifest section, or delete it if it has been fully superseded by `mobile-design-kit-audit-v5/`.

findings.title: templates/memox-dashboard/ example is not listed in readme.md's Index/manifest despite being cited as compliance evidence elsewhere
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/readme.md:71-85 — the Index/manifest section lists `styles.css`, `tokens/`, `components.css`, `components/`, `guidelines/`, `ui_kits/memox-app/`, `fonts/`, `SKILL.md`; no mention of `templates/`.
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/mobile-design-kit-audit-v5/KIT-02-source-of-truth.md row KIT-02-03 cites `templates/memox-dashboard` as PASS evidence ('Template/example dùng instance từ nguồn chuẩn'), i.e. the audit checklist already relies on this folder existing and being canonical, yet it is absent from the kit's own top-level index.
findings.description: The `templates/memox-dashboard/` directory (MemoxDashboard.dc.html, ds-base.js, support.js) is real and used as audit evidence, but a reader navigating only via readme.md's documented Index/manifest would not know it exists.
findings.impact: Minor discoverability gap; does not affect correctness since the folder is legitimate and already referenced by the audit checklist, just not by the kit's own reader-facing index.
findings.recommendation: Add a `templates/` line to readme.md's Index/manifest section for completeness.

findings.title: Today Dashboard docs never define consuming a Statistics summary, contradicting Study Statistics' stated contract and metrics-v1's acceptance criterion
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/study-statistics/README.md:32 "Today Dashboard chỉ consume compact summary, không tự tính metric." and docs/business/study-statistics/metric-formulas-v1.md:31 "Goal, Streak, Today and Statistics reference `metrics-v1`; no surface reimplements formulas."
findings.evidenceB: docs/business/today-dashboard/README.md:26-31 (Cross-object contracts section) lists Due/Learning Progress, paused Session/Study Session, Goal/Streak, recent Deck cards, and create/import handoff — but never mentions Study Statistics or a "compact summary"; a repo-wide grep for "statistic" and "metric-formulas-v1"/"metrics-v1" across all 8 docs/business/today-dashboard/*.md files returns zero matches.
findings.description: Study Statistics' README asserts that Today Dashboard consumes a compact statistics summary, and metric-formulas-v1.md's acceptance criteria explicitly require the "Today" surface to reference the metrics-v1 formula contract so no surface reimplements formulas. However, none of the 8 Today Dashboard flow files (README, load-today-dashboard.md, refresh-today-projections.md, handle-caught-up-today.md, etc.) define what a "compact summary" would contain, how it differs from the Goal/Streak projections Today already reads directly, or reference metrics-v1 at all. This is documentation drift between two aggregates' specs, not a business-vs-design conflict.
findings.impact: An implementer building the Today Dashboard aggregation layer has no contract for a Statistics-derived summary field, so they will either omit it (leaving Study Statistics' stated contract unfulfilled) or invent an undocumented shape for it, and metrics-v1's own acceptance criterion #3 ("Today ... reference metrics-v1") cannot currently be satisfied by the existing Today Dashboard docs.
findings.recommendation: Either add a short 'Consumes compact Statistics summary (see metrics-v1)' line to today-dashboard/README.md's Cross-object contracts section and define what that summary contains (or clarify it's future/deferred), or remove the claim from study-statistics/README.md and metric-formulas-v1.md's acceptance criteria if Today intentionally does not consume Statistics in v1.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: Today Dashboard's Cross-object contracts section and Study Statistics' README/metrics-v1 acceptance criteria agree on whether/what compact summary Today consumes from Statistics, with a concrete reference or explicit deferral.

findings.title: Study Goal and Study Streak each independently re-specify local-day/timezone/DST boundary rules without cross-referencing each other or metrics-v1's canonical localDayId
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/study-streak/README.md:5 "...Dùng local-day contract của Study Goal nhưng không phụ thuộc Goal enabled/met." (claims Streak reuses Goal's local-day contract)
findings.evidenceB: docs/business/study-streak/handle-streak-boundary.md (entire file, e.g. lines 7-11 principles and lines 27-32 boundary decision table) fully re-derives calendar/timezone/DST/manual-clock rollover rules with no reference to Study Goal or to metric-formulas-v1.md's `localDayId` definition (metric-formulas-v1.md:7); docs/business/study-goal/handle-goal-day-boundary.md is likewise fully self-contained with near-identical rules (lines 7-11, edge table lines 44-53) and also never references metrics-v1's localDayId or study-streak's boundary file.
findings.description: Study Streak's README states it reuses Study Goal's local-day contract, and metric-formulas-v1.md separately defines a canonical `localDayId` derivation meant to be the single source for day bucketing. In practice, both handle-goal-day-boundary.md and handle-streak-boundary.md independently write out nearly the same set of midnight/DST/manual-clock/timezone-travel rules as first-class content, with no cross-links tying them to metrics-v1's `localDayId` or to each other. This is a structural duplication of a core invariant across two owning flows.
findings.impact: Two independently maintained specifications of the same day-boundary invariant can silently drift (e.g. one is updated for a new DST edge case and the other is not), producing inconsistent day-bucketing behavior between Goal and Streak even though both are supposed to share one contract.
findings.recommendation: Make metric-formulas-v1.md's `localDayId` the single normative definition of local-day resolution, and have both handle-goal-day-boundary.md and handle-streak-boundary.md explicitly reference it (adding a `[metrics-v1](../study-statistics/metric-formulas-v1.md)` link) rather than repeating the full rule set in prose in both places.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: handle-goal-day-boundary.md and handle-streak-boundary.md both cite metrics-v1's localDayId as their normative day-resolution source, and any owner-specific rules are clearly marked as additions rather than restatements.

findings.title: Study Goal's "distinct qualified Cards" framing is ambiguous against the literal goalProgress formula, which can double-count a Card across two sessions in one day
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/study-goal/README.md:28 "Goal v1 đo distinct qualified Cards theo [metrics-v1](../study-statistics/metric-formulas-v1.md); Practice không đóng góp."
findings.evidenceB: docs/business/study-statistics/metric-formulas-v1.md:11 "Daily Goal unit v1 is qualified Cards. `goalProgress = count(distinct sessionId, cardId)` for qualifying sessions in the local day."
findings.description: The plain-language description in study-goal/README.md reads as "the goal counts how many distinct Cards were qualified today," i.e. one increment per physical Card per day. The literal formula in metric-formulas-v1.md counts distinct (sessionId, cardId) pairs, which — combined with `qualifiedCard` already being defined as distinct-per-session (metric-formulas-v1.md:6) — means the same physical Card touched in two separate qualifying sessions on the same local day (e.g. a newLearning session followed later by a same-day relearn/dueReview session) would add 2 to goalProgress, not 1. track-daily-goal.md's own dedup rule ("Parent/Deck scope không double-count same Card activity trong one Session summary", track-daily-goal.md:42) only guards against double-counting within a single session's Deck hierarchy, not across sessions, so it does not resolve the ambiguity.
findings.impact: An implementer following the README's "distinct qualified Cards" description would build a goal counter that dedupes by Card across the whole day (never double counting a Card), while an implementer following the literal formula would allow the same Card to contribute twice if re-engaged in a second qualifying session — producing different Goal progress numbers and different goal-met transition timing for the same underlying study activity.
findings.recommendation: Clarify in metric-formulas-v1.md and study-goal/README.md whether goalProgress is meant to be per-Card-per-day (true distinct Cards) or per-session-distinct-Card summed across sessions, and align the wording so both documents describe the same counting rule explicitly, including the same-Card-multiple-sessions case in the acceptance criteria or state matrix.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: study-goal/README.md and metric-formulas-v1.md state the same goalProgress counting rule for a Card touched in two qualifying sessions on the same local day, and track-daily-goal.md's state matrix/acceptance criteria explicitly cover that case.

findings.title: Handle Insufficient Statistics assumes per-metric "insufficient" thresholds are defined in the formula version, but metrics-v1 only defines a threshold rule for one metric
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/study-statistics/handle-insufficient-statistics.md:7 "Threshold thuộc formula version, không hard-code trong UI." and line 9 "Mỗi metric có thể đủ/thiếu độc lập."
findings.evidenceB: docs/business/study-statistics/metric-formulas-v1.md:21 is the only threshold-like rule in the file: "terminalAccuracy = terminalCorrect / (terminalCorrect + terminalWrong)... If denominator is zero, state is `insufficient`, not 0%." No other metric (activeStudyDurationMs, qualifiedCardCount, sessionCount, streak-related figures) has any stated minimum-sample or insufficiency condition anywhere in metric-formulas-v1.md.
findings.description: handle-insufficient-statistics.md's principle that "threshold belongs to the formula version, not hard-coded in UI" presumes metric-formulas-v1.md is the authoritative source for every metric's insufficient-vs-available boundary. In fact metrics-v1 only specifies an insufficiency rule for terminalAccuracy's zero-denominator case; it gives no rule for when qualifiedCardCount, sessionCount, activeStudyDurationMs, or streak-derived stats should be considered "below threshold" versus a valid zero.
findings.impact: UI and projection-building code cannot correctly implement the "Below threshold → Insufficient guidance" branch (handle-insufficient-statistics.md master flow) for any metric besides terminalAccuracy, since no threshold values or rules exist to hard-code against or version.
findings.recommendation: Extend metric-formulas-v1.md to define an explicit insufficiency rule (or confirm "none — always available once >0 records exist") for each metric that build-study-statistics.md and view-study-statistics.md expose, so handle-insufficient-statistics.md's per-metric independence claim is backed by a concrete, versioned rule for every metric.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: metric-formulas-v1.md lists, for every metric surfaced by Study Statistics, either its insufficiency condition or an explicit statement that it has none.

findings.title: SRS queue-priority ordering appears to contradict the 'Relearn is never injected into an active session' invariant stated in three other canonical files
findings.evidenceA:
docs/business/learning-progress/srs-8-box-policy.md:142-150 — '## 9. Queue priority

Trong một requested scope:

1. Relearn items thuộc current active Session, theo checkpoint order.
2. Due Cards Box 1..7, dueAt tăng dần...
3. New Cards Box 0...

Một Card chỉ xuất hiện một lần trong effective queue.' — this describes ONE combined 'effective queue' that mixes Relearn + Due + New items by priority for a requested scope.
findings.evidenceB: docs/business/study-session/README.md:41 — 'Mastery-round state, Relearn-session queue và due-review-session queue là ba namespace riêng; không dùng Relearn thay cho retry round trong mode và không inject queue mới vào active session.' Also docs/business/study-session/relearn-cards.md:12 and its entry table ('Card becomes due during another active session | Không đổi active queue; future Due Review') and docs/business/learning-progress/surface-due-cards.md:46 — 'Relearn candidate set không phải SRS due state và không được inject vào active session.'
findings.description: srs-8-box-policy.md — the document explicitly declared as the source of truth for the SRS algorithm — describes a single ranked 'effective queue' per requested scope where Relearn items (item 1) sit alongside Due (item 2) and New (item 3) Cards, deduplicated into one list ('Một Card chỉ xuất hiện một lần trong effective queue'). Three other canonical Learning-Progress/Study-Session documents are explicit and repeated that Relearn is architecturally a separate session-type namespace that is never merged into or injected into an active (newLearning/dueReview) session's queue; it only exists as its own immutable snapshot created after a prior session finalizes. As written, an implementer following srs-8-box-policy.md §9 literally could build one shared queue-priority function that mixes relearn candidates into a Dashboard/Study-Deck due+new query, which would violate the explicit no-injection invariant elsewhere. Neither the SRS8-v1 nor ST-SESSION-TYPE-v1 decision tables contain a row that pins down this 'queue priority' behavior with a testable ID, so there is no executable contract resolving the ambiguity. Note: the prior audit report (docs/audits/documentation-remediation-2026-07-18.md) marks both 'SRS safety' and 'Session safety' as 'Closed' with this exact table cited as evidence; this ambiguity was not resolved by that remediation pass.
findings.impact: If implemented literally, the Dashboard/Study-Deck due-card surfacing (surface-due-cards.md) could silently pull unfinalized Relearn items into a live session queue, corrupting the 'stable missed set created only after finalize' guarantee that Relearn, Finalize and Resume all depend on, or conversely the ambiguity could cause two different engineers to implement two incompatible queue-construction functions.
findings.recommendation: Reword srs-8-box-policy.md §9 to explicitly scope the priority ordering per session-type request (e.g. 'when the requested scope is itself a Relearn session, its own checkpoint order is item 1 of its own queue; Due/New ordering below applies only to newLearning/dueReview/Dashboard requests') and add a decision-table row (or amend ST-SESSION-TYPE-v1) that makes the no-injection rule and the per-request-type queue construction into a testable, exact-ID contract.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: srs-8-box-policy.md §9 and the ST-SESSION-TYPE-v1/SRS8-v1 decision tables agree on whether Relearn items can ever appear in the same queue response as Due/New items for the same request, backed by at least one row with an exact ID and an automated test.

findings.title: The hard 5-distinct-meaning-card minimum that blocks New Learning is never surfaced proactively along the create-language-pair → create-deck → create-flashcard happy path
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/deck/study-deck.md:93-95 — 'Guess yêu cầu candidate pool có ít nhất năm Card với năm meaning hiển thị khác nhau... Nếu không đạt Guess minimum, không cho Start' and docs/business/study-session/start-study-session.md:64 decision-table row 'Guess không có ít nhất 5 meaning khác nhau | Chặn Start; yêu cầu thêm Card hoặc chọn scope khác.' Also study-mode/README.md mastery contract requires newLearning to always run the full Review→Match→Guess→Recall→Fill pipeline (ST-TYPE-002 blocks any reduced pipeline).
findings.evidenceB: docs/business/deck/create-deck.md (First-use landing §4, Step 2 success §7, Empty Deck §11) and docs/business/flashcard/create-flashcard.md (Success §6, Acceptance criteria §10) — neither the first-run onboarding copy, the 'Your first deck is ready' callout, nor the 'Card added' success snackbar mentions that at least 5 cards with 5 distinct meanings are required before New Learning can ever be started.
findings.description: Because Guess's distractor pool is scoped strictly to the current session snapshot (guess-card-meaning.md: 'Distractor lấy từ stable candidate pool của toàn bộ session snapshot, không chỉ từ currentRoundCardIds') and newLearning always mandates the full five-stage pipeline including Guess (ST-TYPE-002 forbids dropping/reordering stages), a brand-new user who follows the exact priority flow — create a language pair, create their first deck, add 1-4 flashcards, then try to start studying — is unconditionally blocked from New Learning until they have created at least 5 cards with 5 distinct meanings in that scope. The block message itself exists reactively at Start time ('Guess needs at least 5 cards with different meanings.'), but nothing in the onboarding/create-deck/create-flashcard specs proactively tells the user this threshold exists, so the documented happy path can produce a confusing 'add a card → try to study → blocked → add another card' loop up to 5 times for a first-time user, with no guidance in the docs about how many cards to add before trying to study.
findings.impact: The core 'start learning' priority flow (the flow this review was asked to scrutinize most carefully) has an undocumented dead-end for the most common new-user scenario (adding one or two example cards first). Without a documented proactive nudge, either the implementation will improvise a UX for this (out of contract) or first-run users will hit an unexplained block with no signpost telling them how many cards they need.
findings.recommendation: Add explicit guidance to deck/create-deck.md (post first-card-added state / Empty→Leaf transition) or to the Empty/Leaf Deck composition specs stating the minimum-card guidance copy (e.g. 'Add at least 5 cards to start studying'), and cross-reference deck/study-deck.md's Guess minimum so the requirement is visible before the user hits the reactive block.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: A Deck/Flashcard business doc states, at or before the point a first-time user would attempt Study with fewer than 5 distinct-meaning cards, proactive copy or state that communicates the New-Learning minimum-card requirement.

findings.title: Practice session type never enumerates which Study Modes are actually selectable
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/deck/study-deck.md:85,91 — '`practice` | Eligible scope do user chọn | User chọn đúng một supported mode; không schedule hoặc activate SRS' and 'Practice Mode Picker chỉ hiển thị/enable modes product hỗ trợ.'
findings.evidenceB: docs/business/study-session/start-study-session.md:21,66,89 and docs/business/study-session/README.md:16-19 all repeat that Practice selects 'đúng một mode' / 'exactly one mode' but never list the candidate set (Review/Match/Guess/Recall/Fill, or a subset).
findings.description: Every file that mentions `practice` (deck/study-deck.md, study-session/README.md, start-study-session.md, finalize-study-session.md) agrees Practice picks exactly one mode and does not schedule SRS/Goal/Streak, but none of them state which of the five Study Modes are eligible for Practice selection. study-deck.md defers to 'modes product hỗ trợ' (modes the product supports) without naming them anywhere in the Study Mode or Study Session catalogs.
findings.impact: ST-TYPE-003/004 in the study-session-types decision table reference Practice mode selection as a testable contract, but there is no enumerable list to write a conformance test against — is Review selectable as a Practice mode (it has no pass/fail so 'scheduleSrs=false' is moot for it), can Match run in Practice with only its own local minimums, etc. This is a real testability gap for a scope that IS covered elsewhere by decision tables.
findings.recommendation: Add an explicit table (in study-mode/README.md or study-session/start-study-session.md) enumerating which of Review/Match/Guess/Recall/Fill are valid `practice` selections and any mode-specific minimum for standalone practice use (e.g. does standalone Guess in Practice still require 5 distinct meanings).
findings.acceptanceCriteria: A canonical table lists every Study Mode eligible for `practice` selection, each with any mode-specific minimum, and ST-TYPE-003/004 reference it by row ID.

findings.title: create-deck.md duplicates Add-Card-target-picker and Import-target-picker content verbatim instead of linking to their owning files, violating the Deck README's own single-source-of-truth rule
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/deck/README.md:54-64 (§2 'Ranh giới để tránh trùng lặp') — 'add-content-to-deck.md sở hữu điều kiện Deck có thể nhận card/import; không sở hữu form Card hoặc parser Import... Nếu một quy tắc ảnh hưởng nhiều flow, chỉ một file được chọn làm source of truth. Các file còn lại dẫn link tới quy tắc đó và chỉ mô tả phần thay đổi tại context của mình.'
findings.evidenceB: docs/business/deck/create-deck.md:555-591 (§16 'Add Card target selection') and :593-618 (§17 'Import UI rules') reproduce the same ASCII picker mockup, eligibility copy and error copy already owned by docs/business/deck/add-content-to-deck.md (§4-5) and docs/business/deck/import-deck.md (§5), without a cross-link.
findings.description: create-deck.md re-describes the entire Add-Card target picker (including the identical ASCII mockup 'Korean TOPIK I ... Parent disabled') and the Import flat-cards target rules that add-content-to-deck.md and import-deck.md already own per the Deck README's explicit de-duplication rule. The content is currently consistent (no contradiction found), but the duplication creates a drift risk: a future edit to add-content-to-deck.md's eligibility rules or copy would not automatically propagate to create-deck.md's copy of the same rules.
findings.impact: Maintenance risk only today, not a functional defect; if the two copies drift in a future edit, a Business-vs-Business ambiguity would appear on the exact core 'start learning' surface (Add Card target selection) that the create-deck → create-flashcard priority path depends on.
findings.recommendation: Replace create-deck.md §16-17 with a summary plus explicit link to add-content-to-deck.md / import-deck.md, consistent with how deck/README.md instructs other cross-feature flows (§43-52 'Cross-feature flows initiated from a Deck') to only own entry-point/target/eligibility summaries and link out for detail.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: create-deck.md no longer restates the full Add-Card/Import target-picker eligibility contract; it links to add-content-to-deck.md/import-deck.md for that content, matching the README's stated ownership boundaries.

findings.title: Two Flashcard specs (manage-card-tags.md, view-card-detail.md) are written in English prose with a different structural template than the rest of the Vietnamese-authored Flashcard/Deck/Study-* catalog
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/flashcard/manage-card-tags.md:1-41 and docs/business/flashcard/view-card-detail.md:1-32 are fully English, using an 'Owner/Status' header, an inline invariants table (TAG-001..006) and a 'States and recovery' table, with no Mermaid master-flow diagram.
findings.evidenceB: Every other file in flashcard/, deck/, learning-progress/, study-mode/ and study-session/ (e.g. docs/business/flashcard/create-flashcard.md:1) uses the 'Đặc tả UI/UX hoàn chỉnh' Vietnamese template with a Mermaid master flow, numbered sections and a canonical state matrix.
findings.description: This is the one clear terminology/format inconsistency found across the 52 files reviewed. It does not create a factual contradiction — both files are internally coherent and their invariants (TAG-001..006, Card Detail states) are consistent with the rest of the Flashcard contract — but the differing language and structure stand out against an otherwise uniform corpus, and Flashcard's README (unlike Deck's README) never declares an explicit template exception for these two files the way deck/README.md names create-deck.md as its reference template.
findings.impact: Low; readability/consistency only. Does not block implementation since the content itself is coherent and cross-referenced correctly (flashcard/README.md's flow catalog lists both as 'Đã có').
findings.recommendation: Either convert the two files to the same Vietnamese template used elsewhere for corpus consistency, or add a short note to flashcard/README.md acknowledging these two files intentionally use a lighter-weight contract format (mirroring how deck/README.md explicitly names its template file).
findings.acceptanceCriteria: flashcard/README.md documents (or the two files adopt) a consistent template/language choice with the rest of the Flashcard catalog.

findings.title: Design's Account/Sign-in mock pre-commits to Google + email/password provider and shows live cloud CTAs, contradicting the Business cloud-service-gate
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/account/cloud-service-gate.md:5,9,24-26,33 — "Tier-1 default: local-only; no cloud provider selected" / "No implementation may infer a provider from a UI mock or generic architecture note." / "Until the gate is accepted, cloud CTAs are absent or explicitly labelled unavailable; local data remains fully usable." / "The provider decision is intentionally not made in this document."
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/account-sync/components/SignInCard.jsx:13-21 — renders a primary CTA `MxButton variant="primary" ... node="account/google"` labeled "Sign in with Google" with body copy "Sign in to back up and sync cards across devices. The app still works offline."; and AccountSync.jsx:30-46 additionally renders a full email/password `EmailSignInForm` ("Continue with email") with real autofill-wired `<input>`s, only annotated by a small caption "This feature is in alpha."
findings.description: Business explicitly defers the cloud provider decision and requires cloud CTAs to be either absent or explicitly labelled unavailable until Product/Security formally record the provider, auth mechanisms, encryption, retention, etc. The Design System's account-sync kit does the opposite: it has already inferred and built for a specific provider (Google + email/password) from a UI mock and presents these as live, primary, usable actions — only softened by a small 'alpha' caption, not 'absent or explicitly labelled unavailable'. The design kit's own issue-register.md has no open or fixed item addressing this provider/gate conflict, so it is not known/resolved design debt.
findings.impact: If the Flutter implementation follows the design mock literally, engineering will build a Google-OAuth + email/password sign-in flow before Product/Security have recorded the required decision record mandated by cloud-service-gate.md — exactly the fail-closed violation the gate exists to prevent, and it silently pre-commits the product to a specific vendor.
findings.recommendation: Product Owner must decide one of: (a) formally accept the cloud-service-gate decision record now, explicitly naming Google + email/password as the approved provider/mechanisms and updating cloud-service-gate.md's status accordingly; (b) instruct Design to replace the live sign-in mock with a gated/disabled placeholder per the fail-closed rule until the gate is accepted; or (c) explicitly document the design mock as illustrative-only/non-authoritative for provider choice and exclude it from the v1 Flutter build.

findings.title: account/README.md marks Sign-in/Sync flows unconditionally 'Đã có' while cloud-service-gate.md classifies Account+Sync as a conditional capability
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/account/README.md:16-24 — flow catalog table lists sign-in.md, sign-out.md, sync-local-data.md, resolve-sync-conflict.md, recover-account-session.md all as plain "Đã có" (done), with no conditional marker, while only delete-account.md is annotated "Đã có — Conditional" and cloud-service-gate.md itself is "Deferred for v1".
findings.evidenceB: docs/business/account/cloud-service-gate.md:7 — "Account, ongoing sync and cloud backup are conditional capabilities. Web + Android v1 may ship the local-first learning flow without them."
findings.description: Reading account/README.md alone, a contributor would conclude Sign-in/Sync/Conflict-resolution/Recover-session are complete, unconditional v1 flows. But cloud-service-gate.md explicitly groups 'Account' and 'ongoing sync' together with cloud backup as conditional capabilities. None of sign-in.md/sync-local-data.md/resolve-sync-conflict.md/recover-account-session.md reference cloud-service-gate.md at all (verified by grep — zero cross-references). Cross-checking the WBS (section 14, item 14.2 'Sign-in/out/session recovery') shows it is scheduled without a conditional marker, unlike 14.5 Account deletion — this partially supports account/README's framing, but this reconciliation is not stated anywhere inside the Account object's own docs.
findings.impact: Implementers reading only docs/business/account/ cannot tell whether building Sign-in/Sync is blocked on the cloud-service-gate decision record or approved to proceed now.
findings.recommendation: Add an explicit note in account/README.md's flow catalog (or a status column annotation) clarifying whether sign-in.md/sync-local-data.md/resolve-sync-conflict.md/recover-account-session.md are spec-complete-but-implementation-gated pending cloud-service-gate.md's decision record, consistent with how delete-account.md and manage-cloud-backup.md already carry a 'Conditional' marker.

findings.title: Preferences' word-display 'gender' and 'romanization' toggles reference Card metadata that Flashcard never defines as an owned field
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/preferences/configure-word-display.md:1 — "Flow này chọn cách hiển thị meaning, gender, romanization và supported word metadata mà không sửa Flashcard content."
findings.evidenceB: docs/business/flashcard/README.md and docs/business/flashcard/create-flashcard.md (full read) contain no field named gender or romanization; repo-wide grep across docs/business/ finds these words occurring only inside preferences/configure-word-display.md (and its own README summary line) — never in flashcard/*.md.
findings.description: Preferences (which explicitly does not own content, per business/README.md's ownership table) assumes Flashcard stores per-card 'grammatical gender' and 'romanization' metadata that can be toggled for display. The Design System's Settings.jsx mock independently confirms this is a real intended feature ('Color by gender — Tint terms by grammatical gender', 'Show romanization — Reading under each term'), so it's not a stray idea — but no document in docs/business/flashcard/ (the owning object) defines these as stored Card fields, their validation, or which Language Pairs they apply to.
findings.impact: Without a defined field owner, an implementer building the Flashcard schema has no basis to add gender/romanization data, and Preferences' toggle has nothing concrete to control — risk of an orphaned setting with no effect, or a blocked feature.
findings.recommendation: Add gender/romanization (and any other 'supported word metadata') as explicitly owned optional fields in docs/business/flashcard/ (e.g. create-flashcard.md or a new manage-card-metadata.md), including per-language-pair applicability and how Study Mode/Card Detail render them, then link back from configure-word-display.md.

findings.title: Flashcard's tag-management flow claims tags refresh 'search projections', but Search's owned contract never defines tags as indexed or filterable
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/flashcard/manage-card-tags.md:6,27 (grep-verified) — "Tags are reusable labels attached to Flashcards for organization and search." / step 6: "Refresh Card Detail, list filters and search projections after commit."
findings.evidenceB: docs/business/search/README.md:14-20 (full read) — the search-rank-v1 matched-field priority list is only Card.term → Card.primaryMeaning → Deck.name → accepted Card translation; docs/business/search/filter-search-results.md (full read) only filters by object type/language pair/deck scope/visibility. Tags are not mentioned anywhere in any of the six Search flow files.
findings.description: Flashcard (the owning object for tag semantics) asserts that committing a tag change refreshes 'search projections', implying tags are searchable/filterable. Search (the owning object for query/ranking/filter) never defines a tag matched-field tier or tag filter dimension anywhere in its six flow files. This is a one-sided cross-object contract: the producer (Flashcard) promises data the consumer (Search) never acknowledges receiving.
findings.impact: Ambiguous whether tag search/filter is in v1 scope; if implemented per Flashcard's wording, Search's ranking/filter acceptance criteria and golden fixtures would be incomplete since they don't cover tag matching.
findings.recommendation: Either add a tag matched-field tier to search-rank-v1 and a tag filter to filter-search-results.md with matching acceptance criteria, or correct manage-card-tags.md to stop claiming a search-projection refresh (or explicitly mark tag search as deferred).

findings.title: configure-mode-preferences.md does not scope whether mode enable/disable/reorder applies to the fixed New-Learning pipeline or only to Practice's mode picker
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/README.md:14 (BD-003) — "Session types are newLearning, dueReview, relearn, practice; New Learning runs all five ordered modes, Practice selects one"; reinforced by docs/business/study-session/README.md:17 — "newLearning bắt buộc hoàn tất Review → Match → Guess → Recall → Fill; practice chọn đúng một mode".
findings.evidenceB: docs/business/preferences/configure-mode-preferences.md:1,7-8,39-41 — "Flow này chọn mode availability/default order trong giới hạn eligibility của Study Mode." / "Không thể vô hiệu hóa mọi mode nếu session cần ít nhất một mode." / state matrix mentions "Defaults/custom order, one/all enabled, disable default" with no distinction between newLearning and practice contexts anywhere in the file.
findings.description: BD-003 and study-session/README.md state New Learning is mandatory-and-fixed: it always runs all five modes in a fixed order, not subject to per-mode enable/disable. configure-mode-preferences.md describes a general 'enable/disable/reorder' preference with only the floor rule 'at least one mode must remain enabled' — it never states this preference is scoped to Practice's mode picker only and cannot touch the mandatory New Learning pipeline. A literal reading would let a user disable, e.g., Fill entirely, conflicting with New Learning's mandatory-completion requirement.
findings.impact: Implementer ambiguity: does disabling a mode via Preferences remove it from the New Learning pipeline (breaking BD-003) or only affect Practice's picker list? Plausible source of a real runtime contract violation if unresolved before implementation.
findings.recommendation: Add an explicit line to configure-mode-preferences.md stating that enable/disable/reorder/default-selection only affects the Practice mode picker, and that New Learning's five-stage pipeline is fixed and unaffected by this preference (or, if disabling is meant to also skip New Learning stages, state that explicitly and reconcile with BD-003/study-session's 'bắt buộc hoàn tất' language).

findings.title: Search ranking policy's 'accepted Card translation' term is undefined and doesn't match Flashcard's own vocabulary
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/search/README.md:18 — "...Deck.name → accepted Card translation; sau đó stable tie-break..."
findings.evidenceB: docs/business/flashcard/manage-card-translations.md (full read — the object is uniformly called 'additional translation'/'additional meaning', never 'accepted'); grep across docs/business/ confirms 'accepted' describing translations appears nowhere else.
findings.description: Search's ranking-tier list uses the qualifier 'accepted' for Card translations, but the owning object (Flashcard's manage-card-translations.md) never defines an 'accepted' vs 'unaccepted/pending' state for translations — it only has ordered 'additional translations' that are either saved or in-draft. Unclear whether 'accepted' means 'saved/committed' or implies an approval workflow that doesn't exist elsewhere.
findings.impact: Minor — low risk of misimplementation since translations have no other lifecycle state to confuse this with, but the vocabulary is inconsistent across the Flashcard/Search boundary.
findings.recommendation: Replace 'accepted Card translation' with 'saved additional Card translation' (or manage-card-translations.md's exact term) to keep vocabulary consistent.

findings.title: MxButton: prompt.md claims 'no built-in loading state' while d.ts and jsx fully implement one
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/core/MxButton.prompt.md:13 — "No built-in loading state — the parent disables the button and shows progress while submitting."
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/core/MxButton.d.ts:19 — "Loading / submitting: shows a spinner, sets `aria-busy`, and makes the button inert."; docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/core/MxButton.jsx:9,12-13 implements `btn--loading`, `aria-busy`, spinner and onClick suppression when `loading`; independently corroborated by docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/feedback/MxProgress.prompt.md:17 — "Not as a button's own affordance (MxButton owns its `loading` spinner)."
findings.description: The MxButton prompt.md spec (the authored contract a consumer/implementer is meant to read) explicitly denies a built-in loading state, but the type contract and reference implementation both fully implement one, and a sibling component's own prompt.md (MxProgress) independently confirms the loading state exists on MxButton. This is documentation drift within the design docs, not code vs. business — one of the three artifacts (most likely prompt.md, which appears stale) is out of sync with the other two/three.
findings.impact: A Flutter implementer reading only MxButton.prompt.md (the primary consumer-facing spec) would build a button without the documented loading/aria-busy/spinner behavior, then find it contradicted by the type contract, wasting implementation time and risking two divergent MxButton behaviors across features that read different source files.
findings.recommendation: Update MxButton.prompt.md's States section to document `loading` (spinner, aria-busy, inert, blocked onClick) consistently with the .d.ts and .jsx, removing the 'no built-in loading state' sentence or clarifying whether it is stale guidance that should be deleted.

findings.title: MxChip: prompt.md says disabled is 'not yet a modeled state... planned' while d.ts and jsx fully implement chip--disabled
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/core/MxChip.prompt.md:11 — "Disabled is not yet a modeled state (see constraints matrix / KIT-18-04) — omit or hide an unavailable chip rather than graying it."; line 17 Meta — "disabled state planned — see KIT-18-04."
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/core/MxChip.d.ts:9-10 — `disabled?: boolean` "Unavailable / disabled: inert and dimmed."; docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/core/MxChip.jsx:4,7,10 implements `chip--disabled` class, native `disabled` attribute, `aria-disabled`, and onClick suppression.
findings.description: Same class of drift as MxButton: the prompt.md explicitly instructs implementers to 'omit or hide' rather than gray out an unavailable chip because disabled is supposedly unmodeled, but the type contract and reference jsx already ship a complete disabled implementation with a dedicated CSS modifier class.
findings.impact: An implementer following the prompt.md guidance would build chip-hiding logic instead of using the already-designed disabled visual state, producing inconsistent UX across screens that use the two different guidance sources, and wasting/duplicating already-completed design work.
findings.recommendation: Reconcile MxChip.prompt.md with the shipped disabled state: either document it as a supported state (recommended, since jsx/d.ts already implement it) or remove the disabled prop/CSS from d.ts/jsx if it is intentionally not yet approved for use.

findings.title: MxSegmentedControl: prompt.md says per-segment disabled 'is not modeled' while d.ts and jsx fully implement it
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/core/MxSegmentedControl.prompt.md:11 — "Per-segment disabled is not modeled (see constraints matrix)."
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/core/MxSegmentedControl.d.ts:6-7 — `MxSegment.disabled?: boolean` "Unavailable / disabled option: inert and dimmed."; docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/core/MxSegmentedControl.jsx:13,16 reads `s.disabled`, sets native `disabled`, `aria-disabled`, and guards `onChange` from firing.
findings.description: Third instance of the same drift pattern: the prompt.md's States prose has not been updated to reflect the per-segment disabled capability that is already present and functioning in the type contract and reference implementation.
findings.impact: Implementers following only the prose spec would not know a disabled segment option is available and might build ad-hoc handling (e.g. hiding a segment) instead of using the supported disabled field on MxSegment.
findings.recommendation: Update the States section of MxSegmentedControl.prompt.md to document per-segment disabled consistently with the .d.ts/.jsx, or clarify why the prose still calls it unmodeled if there is a reason it should not be used yet.

findings.title: MxTextField: prompt.md says read-only 'is not yet a distinct state... planned' while d.ts and jsx fully implement readOnly
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/core/MxTextField.prompt.md:30 — "Read-only is not yet a distinct state (see constraints matrix / KIT-18-02)."; line 36 Meta — "read-only state planned — see KIT-18-02."
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/core/MxTextField.d.ts:29-30 — `readOnly?: boolean` "Read-only: value is shown and focusable/selectable but not editable (distinct from `disabled`)."; docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/core/MxTextField.jsx:10,18,25 implements `field--readonly` class and passes `readOnly` through to the `<input>`/`<textarea>`.
findings.description: Fourth and clearest instance of the pattern: the .d.ts comment explicitly distinguishes readOnly from disabled with detailed semantics, and the jsx implements it fully, yet the prompt.md States section and Meta both still describe read-only as a future/planned item tied to an open issue (KIT-18-02).
findings.impact: Same as above — implementers following the prose spec would believe read-only fields must be faked with disabled (losing focusability/selectability semantics that disabled does not provide), diverging from the already-implemented, more correct readOnly behavior.
findings.recommendation: Update MxTextField.prompt.md's States section and Meta line to document readOnly as shipped, or explain why KIT-18-02 is still open despite the field already existing in the type contract and reference implementation.

findings.title: MxLink: disabled state is fully typed and implemented but entirely absent from the prompt.md States section
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/core/MxLink.prompt.md:17 — "States — default, hover, `:focus-visible` ring, pressed; sizes default / `sm`; renders `<button>` or, with `href`, `<a>`." (no mention of disabled anywhere in the file)
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/core/MxLink.d.ts:15-17 — `disabled?: boolean` with a detailed two-sentence doc comment describing distinct `<button disabled>` vs `<a aria-disabled + tabIndex=-1>` behavior; docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/core/MxLink.jsx:14,19-23 implements this exact dual-form logic.
findings.description: Unlike the four items above, this is not a direct contradiction (the prompt.md doesn't say disabled is unsupported) but a silent omission: a carefully-designed, form-aware disabled behavior (real disabled attribute for the button form, aria-disabled+tabIndex=-1 for the anchor form) exists in the type contract and implementation but is never mentioned in the component's own spec document.
findings.impact: A reader relying on the prompt.md as the source of truth for what states MxLink supports would not discover the disabled capability or its two different accessible-implementation strategies for <button> vs <a>, and might re-implement disabling incorrectly (e.g. only setting a CSS class, which would leave an <a> still focusable and activatable).
findings.recommendation: Add a disabled bullet to the States section of MxLink.prompt.md describing the button-vs-anchor accessible disabling strategy already documented in the .d.ts.

findings.title: MxSwitch: prompt.md says ariaLabel 'is required' but the type contract marks it optional/'strongly recommended'
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/core/MxSwitch.prompt.md:12 — "No visible text — `ariaLabel` is required; the row's visible label lives outside the switch and carries all i18n."
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/core/MxSwitch.d.ts:8 — `ariaLabel?: string;` "Accessible label (the switch has no visible text). Strongly recommended."
findings.description: Minor wording tension between 'required' (prompt.md) and an optional prop documented as merely 'strongly recommended' (d.ts). Neither the .d.ts type nor the .jsx (which passes ariaLabel straight to aria-label with no runtime validation/warning) actually enforces the requirement.
findings.impact: Low — a caller could easily ship an MxSwitch with no ariaLabel and no visible external label, producing an unlabeled control for screen-reader users, and neither the type system nor the component would catch it despite prompt.md's 'required' language.
findings.recommendation: Align the wording — either make ariaLabel required whenever no accessible external label is guaranteed (not enforceable by prop typing alone, so document the burden on the caller more strongly), or soften prompt.md's 'required' to 'strongly recommended' to match the .d.ts.

findings.title: MxList's reference implementation lives in surfaces/, not core/, alongside its .d.ts/.prompt.md, and is unreferenced by any demo card
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/core/MxList.d.ts and docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/core/MxList.prompt.md exist in core/, but there is no core/MxList.jsx.
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/surfaces/MxList.jsx contains the actual implementation (verified functionally consistent: default gap var(--memox-space-3), gap override, node prop all match core/MxList.d.ts and core/MxList.prompt.md). Neither core/core.card.html nor surfaces/surfaces.card.html includes a <script type="text/babel-src" src="MxList.jsx"> tag, so MxList is not wired into any gallery demo.
findings.description: This is a file-organization inconsistency: every other core/ component (Avatar, Badge, Button, Chip, Link, SegmentedControl, Switch, TextField) keeps its .d.ts/.jsx/.prompt.md triad co-located in core/, but MxList's .jsx is filed under surfaces/ instead, with no cross-reference from either directory's .card.html to load and demo it.
findings.impact: Low functional risk (the implementation itself matches its spec), but it breaks the 'read the three co-located files' navigation convention used throughout the kit, is easy to miss during future edits/audits, and MxList has no visual verification surface in either component-card gallery.
findings.recommendation: Move MxList.jsx into core/ next to its .d.ts/.prompt.md (or, if it's intentionally treated as a surfaces-layer primitive, move the .d.ts/.prompt.md into surfaces/ instead), and add it to a .card.html gallery for visual verification.

findings.title: feedback/ has no *.card.html gallery, unlike core/, navigation/, and surfaces/ — Banner, Dialog, Menu, Progress, Sheet have no interactive reference/demo surface
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/core/core.card.html, docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/navigation/navigation.card.html, and docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/surfaces/surfaces.card.html each exist and load their respective directory's Mx* .jsx sources into a live demo page.
findings.evidenceB: A recursive search for *.html under components/ returns only those three files; docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/feedback/ contains only .d.ts/.jsx/.prompt.md for MxBanner, MxDialog, MxMenu, MxProgress, MxSheet — no feedback.card.html.
findings.description: The feedback/ directory is the only one of the four component groups reviewed that lacks a .card.html gallery card wiring its components into a live, runnable demo via _ds_runtime.js. This is notable because feedback/ contains the components with the most complex documented interaction contracts in this batch (focus trap, live-region roles, dismissal, drag handle) — exactly the components where a runnable demo would be most valuable for catching the kind of implementation gaps flagged in C2-009/C2-010 below.
findings.impact: There is no visual/interactive artifact to sanity-check MxBanner/MxDialog/MxMenu/MxProgress/MxSheet against their prompt.md contracts (tone/role mapping, focus trap, dismissal, item states, determinate/indeterminate rendering) the way core/navigation/surfaces components can be. This reduces the design kit's own ability to catch drift like C2-009/C2-010 before it reaches the Flutter implementation.
findings.recommendation: Add a feedback.card.html gallery card (mirroring core.card.html's structure) that loads MxBanner/MxDialog/MxMenu/MxProgress/MxSheet via _ds_runtime.js and exercises their documented states.

findings.title: MxDialog: documented 'focus returns to the opener on close' behavior is not implemented in the reference jsx
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/feedback/MxDialog.d.ts:12 — "on close, focus returns to the opener."; docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/feedback/MxDialog.prompt.md:9 — "On close, focus returns to the element that opened the dialog."
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/feedback/MxDialog.jsx:29-35 — the only useEffect sets panelRef.current.focus() on open and attaches an Escape keydown listener; there is no code anywhere in the file that captures document.activeElement before opening or restores it on close/unmount.
findings.description: The focus-trap contract documented identically in both the .d.ts header comment and the prompt.md promises focus restoration to the triggering element on close, and this promise is not caveated as solely 'host-app enforced' any more than the focus-on-open or Escape-handling behaviors are — both of which ARE implemented directly in this same reference file. The asymmetry (open-focus and Escape implemented, close-restore not) suggests an incomplete reference implementation rather than an intentional delegation boundary.
findings.impact: A keyboard or screen-reader user who opens a dialog and then dismisses it loses their prior focus position (focus typically falls back to <body>), which is a WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order) / general modal-accessibility regression; an implementer copying this reference as their behavioral guide would ship the same gap.
findings.recommendation: Add focus-restoration to MxDialog.jsx: capture the pre-open document.activeElement in a ref when the dialog opens, and call .focus() on it in the effect's cleanup / when open transitions to false.

findings.title: MxSheet: documented 'focus returns to the opener on close' behavior is not implemented in the reference jsx
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/feedback/MxSheet.d.ts:12 — "Focus moves into the sheet on open and returns to the opener on close."; docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/feedback/MxSheet.prompt.md:8 — "Focus moves into the sheet on open and returns to the opener on close."
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/feedback/MxSheet.jsx:26-32 — same pattern as MxDialog: the effect only calls sheetRef.current.focus() on open and adds an Escape listener; no capture/restore of the previously focused element.
findings.description: Identical gap to C2-009, in the sibling MxSheet component, which shares the same dismissal/focus contract description verbatim in its own docs.
findings.impact: Same as MxDialog — keyboard/AT users lose focus context after dismissing a bottom sheet (e.g. the card-actions sheet in the prompt.md example), regressing focus order for a very common interaction (opening/closing action sheets).
findings.recommendation: Apply the same fix as MxDialog: store the pre-open active element and restore focus to it when the sheet closes.

findings.title: MxSegmentedControl declares role=radiogroup/radio but the reference jsx implements no roving-tabindex or arrow-key navigation
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/core/MxSegmentedControl.jsx:8,16 — the wrapper uses role="radiogroup" and each segment button uses role="radio" with aria-checked.
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/core/MxSegmentedControl.jsx:16 — each <button> has no tabIndex management (all remain individually tab-stoppable by default) and there is no keydown handler for ArrowLeft/ArrowRight; docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/core/MxSegmentedControl.prompt.md:11 only documents 'segment :focus-visible' and, for RN, 'roving focus' — it does not describe or disclaim the web keyboard model the way MxMenu's docs explicitly do (compare docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/feedback/MxMenu.d.ts:27 'Keyboard: items are natively focusable buttons').
findings.description: The WAI-ARIA Radio Group pattern that role="radiogroup"/role="radio" implies expects a single Tab stop into the group with ArrowLeft/ArrowRight (or Up/Down) moving the checked state and roving tabindex between options. The reference implementation instead makes every segment its own native, individually-focusable <button> with no arrow-key handling, which is a materially different (and non-conformant) keyboard model for the ARIA role it declares — and unlike MxMenu (which explicitly documents its simplified 'natively focusable buttons' keyboard model as an intentional deviation), MxSegmentedControl's docs are silent on this, leaving it ambiguous whether this is a known simplification or an oversight.
findings.impact: Keyboard and screen-reader users relying on the standard radiogroup arrow-key pattern (many AT users tab to the group once, then arrow between options) will instead Tab through every segment individually, which is unexpected given the announced role="radio" semantics and could confuse assistive-technology users about how many stops the control has.
findings.recommendation: Either implement roving tabIndex/arrow-key handling to match the ARIA radiogroup pattern the roles declare, or explicitly document (as MxMenu does) that the web reference intentionally uses a simplified native-button keyboard model instead of full roving focus, so implementers aren't left guessing which behavior is the intended contract.

findings.title: Multiple guideline/governance docs still instruct engineers to build against React Native / Expo APIs despite the ratified Flutter pivot
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/SCOPE.md:1-4 — "Product decisions ratified 2026-07-18... supersedes the historical React Native / phone-portrait assumptions"
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/guidelines/system-ui.md:9-11,65-69 — "this governs the RN runtime"... "Configure once via `expo-status-bar`... and `expo-navigation-bar`... See the Expo v57 docs before wiring"; also guidelines/permissions-capabilities.md:51,61-62 (`Linking.openSettings()`, "Check the Expo v57 permissions APIs (expo-camera, expo-notifications, expo-local-authentication) before wiring"); guidelines/keyboard-focus-order.md:119 ("production maps these to React Native `enterKeyHint` / `inputMode` / `onSubmitEditing`"); guidelines/navigation-overlays.md:5 ("Production RN navigation implements these rules"); tokens/spacing.css:33,38 ("Production RN maps this to the SafeAreaView top/bottom inset"); governance/asset-export-spec.md:15-16 ("RN font asset", "RN icon font in production"); governance/exception-register.md:20 ("RN-web rasterizes text differently")
findings.description: SCOPE.md explicitly states the Product Owner's 2026-07-18 decision supersedes React Native assumptions and that production is Flutter (mapped via ThemeData/ThemeExtensions and Mx* widgets). However several guideline and governance files that are dated Current/authoritative were not updated: system-ui.md's entire 'Where this is set' implementation section names only RN/Expo APIs (SafeAreaView doesn't exist in Flutter; StatusBar barStyle / expo-status-bar / expo-navigation-bar / Expo v57 have no Flutter analog) with zero Flutter mapping given anywhere in that file. permissions-capabilities.md tells engineers to check Expo permission packages before wiring a Flutter feature. keyboard-focus-order.md's dismiss-behaviour matrix explicitly maps to React Native props. This is documentation drift: the design rules themselves (e.g. icon-mode-by-surface-luminance, dismiss-without-submit) remain framework-agnostic and valid, but the concrete 'where/how to implement' guidance sends Flutter engineers to non-existent packages.
findings.impact: An engineer implementing system UI theming, permission handling, or keyboard-dismiss behavior in Flutter and following these guideline docs literally will look for APIs (SafeAreaView, expo-status-bar, Linking.openSettings(), RN enterKeyHint) that do not exist in a Flutter codebase, causing wasted implementation time or incorrect ad-hoc improvisation with no documented Flutter equivalent (e.g. SystemChrome.setSystemUIOverlayStyle, app_settings package, TextInputAction).
findings.recommendation: Sweep all guideline/governance docs for RN/Expo-specific API references introduced before the 2026-07-18 Flutter rebaseline and add/replace with the equivalent Flutter API, or explicitly mark the section as needing a Flutter mapping (open gap) rather than presenting stale RN guidance as the current contract.

findings.title: Contribution/acceptance gates present npm verification tooling as operative, while coverage-report.md (same rebaseline) says that tooling does not exist in this repository
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/governance/CONTRIBUTING.md:77-89 — "A contribution merges only when: 1. Adherence lint passes... 2. Kit verification passes — `npm run verify:ui-kit`... 3. Parity gate passes... `npm run parity:gate`"; same commands repeated as hard gates in governance/acceptance-criteria.md (T4/C6/P4/P5 Legend), governance/versioning.md, governance/duplicate-scan.md, governance/component-promotion.md, and governance/theme-onboarding.md (steps 2-4)
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/governance/coverage-report.md:24-31 — "Older documents cited these unavailable paths/commands: `package.json` / `npm run verify:ui-kit`; `tool/ui_kit_shots/registry.mjs`... `tool/parity/*` and `tool/a11y/*`. Those artifacts are not present in the current repository. Their historical reported results are not current release evidence and must not be presented as PASS."
findings.description: Six governance documents (CONTRIBUTING, acceptance-criteria, versioning, duplicate-scan, component-promotion, theme-onboarding) present `npm run verify:ui-kit`, `npm run parity:gate`, `npm run lint`, and `node tool/ui_kit_shots/contrast.mjs` as real, currently-runnable, mandatory merge gates with no caveat. coverage-report.md — written and dated as part of the same 2026-07-18 rebaseline batch — explicitly states these exact paths/commands are absent from the current repository and must not be cited as passing. The two sets of governance docs contradict each other on whether a contributor can literally satisfy the stated Definition of Done.
findings.impact: A contributor following CONTRIBUTING.md's literal instructions has no way to satisfy the stated merge gates (the commands do not exist), and nothing in CONTRIBUTING.md/acceptance-criteria.md itself warns of this, so the mandatory-sounding gates are silently unsatisfiable as written.
findings.recommendation: Add a cross-reference/caveat in CONTRIBUTING.md, acceptance-criteria.md, versioning.md, duplicate-scan.md, component-promotion.md and theme-onboarding.md pointing to coverage-report.md's tooling-absence disclosure, or restore the missing tool/package.json infrastructure, or explicitly downgrade these gates to 'not currently enforceable — manual review only' until the tooling exists.

findings.title: Adaptive-layout tokens added for the Flutter Tier-1 contract are not registered in _ds_manifest.json, violating the kit's own Token acceptance criterion T6
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/tokens/spacing.css:23-31 — `--memox-gutter-medium`, `--memox-gutter-expanded`, `--memox-content-width-reading`, `--memox-content-width-study`, `--memox-content-width-list`, `--memox-content-width-dashboard`, referenced as load-bearing values by guidelines/flutter-adaptive-layout.md §2 ("Screen gutter | `--memox-gutter` | `--memox-gutter-medium` | `--memox-gutter-expanded`" etc.)
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/governance/acceptance-criteria.md:27 — "T6 | Registered in `_ds_manifest.json` token inventory | manifest diff"; verified via `_ds_manifest.json`'s `tokens` array (265 entries) containing none of the six token names above
findings.description: The six adaptive-layout width/gutter tokens that flutter-adaptive-layout.md (dated Current, 2026-07-18) depends on as its canonical gutter/max-width contract are absent from _ds_manifest.json's token inventory, even though the manifest does include the older/legacy `--memox-appbar-lg-height` and dozens of other tokens. This is a concrete violation of the kit's self-declared Token Definition-of-Done (T6).
findings.impact: Any manifest-driven tooling (design-sync, coverage/parity diffing, the omelette lint config's token allowlist) that trusts `_ds_manifest.json` as the token source of truth will not know these adaptive tokens exist, and a reviewer checking T6 compliance per acceptance-criteria.md would correctly fail this addition, yet it already ships as part of the canonical Flutter adaptive contract.
findings.recommendation: Regenerate/update `_ds_manifest.json`'s token inventory to include the six `--memox-gutter-medium/-expanded` and `--memox-content-width-*` tokens (and re-run whatever design-sync-cli produced the manifest) before treating the adaptive-layout contract as fully governance-compliant.

findings.title: Screen/state counts are inconsistent across governance docs from the same 2026-07-18 rebaseline
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/readme.md:81 — "ui_kits/memox-app/ — the compact-profile screen gallery (27 screens × 211 registered historical states...)"; docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/governance/OWNERS.md:43 — "Per-screen feature composites (26 screens)"
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/governance/coverage-report.md:11-13 — "Screen manifest | 27 specs..."; "Active registered state count | 213 after removing 3 conversion states and adding Recall timeout + 4 Today states"
findings.description: readme.md's screen count (27) matches coverage-report.md, but its state count (211) does not match coverage-report.md's current figure (213) even though both are dated/part of the same 2026-07-18 rebaseline (211 appears to be the pre-rebaseline count: 211 - 3 conversion + 1 timeout + 4 Today = 213). Separately, OWNERS.md still cites the old pre-rebaseline screen count of 26 (matching CHANGELOG.md's historical [v4] frozen-baseline entry, which is correctly labeled historical) rather than the current 27.
findings.impact: Low — purely a documentation-quality/consistency issue; a reader cross-referencing readme.md against coverage-report.md or OWNERS.md gets slightly different inventory numbers, which undermines confidence in the numbers being carefully maintained.
findings.recommendation: Update readme.md's state count to 213 and OWNERS.md's screen count to 27 to match the current coverage-report.md rebaseline figures.

findings.title: A 'deck-detail' feature (DeckHeader, DeckMenu, DeleteConfirmDialog, FlashcardRow, SubDeckCard) is referenced by the lint config and audit dts list but absent from the current component/readme index with no deprecation-register entry
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/audit/_dts_list.txt:15-19 lists `ui_kits/memox-app/_features/deck-detail/components/{DeckHeader,DeckMenu,DeleteConfirmDialog,FlashcardRow,SubDeckCard}.d.ts`; docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/_adherence.oxlintrc.json:27-28 still restricts imports from `ui_kits/memox-app/_features/deck-detail/**` and `.../deck-detail/components/**`; the historical docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/audit/UI-UX Audit.html:199-215 describes a 13-state 'Deck Detail' screen as "the deepest coverage in the kit"
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/readme.md's "Kit composites (ui_kits/memox-app)" index (lines 95-123) and `_ds_manifest.json`'s `components`/`cards` arrays enumerate every current feature (account-sync, dashboard, export, flashcard-editor, flashcard-list, import, library ×2, match-mode, mode-picker, recall-mode, fill-mode, player, reminder, review-mode, search, settings, statistics, study-result, study-session, theme, _shared) with no mention of `deck-detail` anywhere; governance/deprecation-policy.md's deprecation register (lines 36-40) has no row for a `deck-detail` feature or its components
findings.description: The lint config and an audit artifact both still reference a `deck-detail` feature/screen with five named components that do not appear anywhere in the currently-documented kit index (readme.md Kit composites list, `_ds_manifest.json` components array). The kit's own governance rule (deprecation-policy.md) requires that any renamed/removed artifact get a deprecation-register row with replacement/reason/since/removal-target — no such row exists for `deck-detail` or its components, so either the readme/manifest index is silently incomplete (a documentation gap) or the feature was retired without following the kit's own deprecation process.
findings.impact: A reader relying on readme.md's Kit composites list or `_ds_manifest.json` to understand what screens/composites currently exist would not learn that `deck-detail` (or its replacement, if one exists) is part of the kit, and the lint config continues to reference a feature path that may no longer be current, risking confusion about whether Deck Detail is a live screen, was merged into Library, or was dropped.
findings.recommendation: Either add `deck-detail` to readme.md's Kit composites index and `_ds_manifest.json` if it is still a live screen, or add a `deprecation-policy.md` register row (and clean the stale `_adherence.oxlintrc.json` pattern / `_dts_list.txt` entries) if it was folded into `library` as the CHANGELOG's Deck-model unification entry implies for `subdeck-list`.

findings.title: readme.md's token index list omits half the token files that styles.css actually imports
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/readme.md:74 — "`tokens/` — `colors.css` (light+dark roles), `typography.css` (+ `@font-face`), `spacing.css`, `radius.css`, `elevation.css`."
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/styles.css:7-18 `@import`s eleven token files: colors, typography, spacing, component, opacity, radius, stroke, size, icon-size, elevation, motion, high-contrast — i.e. readme.md's index is missing `component.css`, `opacity.css`, `stroke.css`, `size.css`, `icon-size.css`, `motion.css`, and `high-contrast.css`
findings.description: The readme's own 'Index / manifest' section, meant to orient a new reader to the tokens directory, lists only 5 of the 11 token files that are actually part of the shipped system and imported by styles.css.
findings.impact: Low — a reader using only the readme's index would not discover that component-dimension, opacity, stroke, size, icon-size, motion, or high-contrast tokens exist, though they are fully documented in their own files and used throughout components.css.
findings.recommendation: Update readme.md's tokens/ index bullet to list all eleven files styles.css imports.

findings.title: MxIconButton aria-label fallback leaks the raw icon ligature, contradicting its own d.ts contract
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/navigation/MxIconButton.d.ts:10-15 — "Accessible label — REQUIRED. The button has no visible text; a screen reader must not fall back to the raw icon ligature (e.g. \"arrow_back\", \"more_horiz\"). Use a human label..."
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/navigation/MxIconButton.jsx:10 — `aria-label={ariaLabel || icon}` — when the caller omits ariaLabel, the component falls back to the raw ligature string, exactly the pattern the d.ts forbids.
findings.description: MxIconButton is the shared icon-only action control used throughout the app (back, close, more, filter, notification bell, etc., per navigation.card.html and MxContextualAppBar.jsx). Its own type contract explicitly names this exact failure mode as unacceptable and gives the same example ligatures, yet the reference implementation performs precisely that fallback instead of e.g. omitting aria-label, throwing in dev mode, or requiring the prop at the type level. Since ariaLabel is typed as required but not enforced at runtime, any consumer that forgets it silently ships an inaccessible or nonsensical announcement (e.g. "more_horiz", "local_fire_department") to screen reader users on a component used dozens of times across the app.
findings.impact: Screen reader users hit raw, sometimes unreadable Material Symbol ligature names announced in place of a human label on any icon button where a developer omits ariaLabel; because the frozen contract provides no runtime guard, this failure mode is silent and will recur across every screen that reuses MxIconButton.
findings.recommendation: Either (a) remove the silent ligature fallback so a missing ariaLabel fails loudly (dev-time warning/throw) rather than leaking the ligature, or (b) explicitly document in the .d.ts/.prompt.md that omission degrades to the ligature and instruct implementers to fail a lint/dev-assert on missing ariaLabel — the code and its own doc cannot keep contradicting each other.

findings.title: MxFab round-FAB aria-label fallback also leaks the raw icon ligature despite being documented as REQUIRED
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/navigation/MxFab.d.ts:9 — "Accessible name — REQUIRED for an icon-only FAB (no visible label)." and MxFab.prompt.md:15 — "Don't ship a round FAB without ariaLabel."
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/navigation/MxFab.jsx:11 — `aria-label={ariaLabel || (!label ? icon : undefined)}` — an icon-only (round) FAB with no ariaLabel silently gets the raw icon ligature (e.g. "shuffle", "add") as its accessible name instead of failing or omitting the label.
findings.description: Same anti-pattern as MxIconButton (finding C3-001), on the app's single primary action control. The component's own doc marks ariaLabel REQUIRED and its sibling MxIconButton.d.ts explicitly calls this exact fallback pattern unacceptable, yet MxFab.jsx implements it anyway.
findings.impact: A round FAB shipped without ariaLabel (a realistic oversight since it's not type-enforced) announces the bare ligature to screen reader users on the app's primary per-screen action button.
findings.recommendation: Align the round-FAB fallback behavior with the stated REQUIRED contract — fail loudly in dev rather than silently substituting the ligature, consistent with how MxIconButton.d.ts describes the ideal behavior for icon-only controls.

findings.title: MxContextualAppBar.prompt.md documents variants and a prop that do not exist in the frozen d.ts/jsx, and self-contradicts the file's own scroll-behavior description
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/surfaces/MxContextualAppBar.prompt.md:5-6,9-10,19,24,33 — examples use `variant="root-contextual" context="Saturday · 27 Jun"`, `variant="root-standard"`, `variant="focused"`, and Rules state "`root-contextual`: show the context label ONLY at top and the destination title ONLY when scrolled — never both", while States says "(`root-contextual`/`root-standard`/`focused` are `root`/`modal` compositions)".
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/surfaces/MxContextualAppBar.d.ts:3-8 — `MxContextualAppBarVariant` union is exactly `'root'|'nested'|'search'|'selection'|'modal'` (no `root-contextual`/`root-standard`/`focused`, no `context` prop declared anywhere in MxContextualAppBarProps at lines 10-31); and d.ts:12-13/34-36 plus jsx:46-60 confirm root/nested/search are FLAT at every scroll position (`top == scrolled`) with no scroll-driven visual change at all — directly contradicting the Rules-section scroll-collapse behavior quoted in evidenceA. The .jsx (lines 40-84) never reads a `context` prop and has no branch for any of the three aliased variant strings; passing them just yields an unstyled `cappbar--root-contextual` class with the default title-only branch, so the documented "context label collapses to title on scroll" behavior is not implemented anywhere.
findings.description: MxContextualAppBar is described as "the ONE app bar for every screen" (frozen, additive-only).
