Its prompt.md — the file meant to guide correct usage/generation — repeatedly uses variant names and a `context` prop with no definition anywhere in the actual type contract or implementation, and describes scroll-collapse behavior that the same document's own States section says was explicitly removed ("top == scrolled", collapsed prop "NO LONGER changes the bar visually"). A consumer following the prompt.md's own primary example (root-contextual, the Dashboard/Today root bar) cannot get the documented behavior from the frozen component at all.
findings.impact: Any implementer (human or AI-assisted) following MxContextualAppBar.prompt.md's own root/dashboard example will pass an undeclared variant and an undeclared prop that TypeScript would reject and that the runtime silently ignores, ending up with a plain always-showing title bar instead of the documented context-then-title collapse — a defect that would surface on one of the most visible screens (Today/Dashboard) and is invisible in code review because it fails silently.
findings.recommendation: Either implement the `root-contextual`/`root-standard`/`focused` compositions and `context` prop for real (extend the variant union and jsx), or strike these examples/rules from MxContextualAppBar.prompt.md and replace them with the 5 variants the d.ts/jsx actually support, so the frozen contract and its usage doc agree.

findings.title: Rail-vs-bottom-nav breakpoint required by the adaptive-layout guideline has no home in MxScaffold's or MxBottomNav's frozen contract
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/guidelines/flutter-adaptive-layout.md:39-42 — "Compact: four destinations in `MxBottomNav`... Medium/expanded: the same destinations render as a navigation rail owned by `MxScaffold`. The public destination ids and `data-mx-node=\"shell/bottom-nav\"` remain stable even when the visual presentation is a rail."
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/surfaces/MxScaffold.d.ts:1-24 (no width-profile/rail prop of any kind; scaffold is described only as "The root phone-screen frame" at a fixed 390x844 startingPoint viewport) and docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/navigation/MxBottomNav.d.ts:1-17 / MxBottomNav.jsx:1-24 (renders unconditionally as `<nav className="bottom-nav">`, no orientation/profile prop, no rail markup or class anywhere) and MxBottomNav.prompt.md:1-17 (States section lists only per-item active/inactive/focus — no mention of a rail, Medium, Expanded, or window-width concept at all).
findings.description: guidelines/flutter-adaptive-layout.md is marked "canonical... Status: Current" and explicitly says it "closes the specification portion of KIT-11-05, KIT-32...36", i.e. it is meant to be an authoritative, implementation-ready spec. It assigns ownership of the Medium/Expanded navigation-rail presentation to MxScaffold, and requires the underlying MxBottomNav destination ids/node to remain stable through that visual swap. But MxScaffold and MxBottomNav — both explicitly "frozen, additive-only" — contain zero variant, prop, class, or state addressing width profiles, a rail, or any transformation of the bottom-nav's presentation. There is no defined rail visual (active-indicator style, icon/label layout, collapse/expand, focus order) anywhere in this component group.
findings.impact: A Flutter implementer building the Medium/Expanded navigation rail required by the adaptive-layout guideline has no design-system-specified widget, class, or prop to build against for MxScaffold/MxBottomNav — they must invent the rail's visual/interaction design from scratch, risking inconsistency with the rest of the frozen kit and rework once the design team does specify it.
findings.recommendation: Add a rail-specific artifact (e.g. an `MxNavRail` component or an explicit `profile`/`layout` prop on MxScaffold documented in its d.ts/prompt.md) that satisfies guidelines/flutter-adaptive-layout.md §3, or amend §3 to point at an existing component if the rail is deliberately intended to be an unfrozen, feature-owned composition — as written, no such component exists in the kit.

findings.title: MxIconButton.jsx implements a `disabled` prop that its own d.ts omits and its prompt.md says doesn't exist
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/navigation/MxIconButton.prompt.md:11 — "No disabled variant is modeled — omit the action instead of showing a dead control." and MxIconButton.d.ts:1-16 (no `disabled` field in MxIconButtonProps).
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/navigation/MxIconButton.jsx:4,10 — `function MxIconButton({ ..., onClick, ariaLabel, disabled = false })` ... `disabled={disabled}` is wired straight to the native button.
findings.description: The implementation silently supports a disabled state the type contract doesn't declare and the usage doc explicitly disclaims. This is the reverse of the usual drift (undocumented capability rather than missing one), but is still a genuine three-way mismatch within a single component's own file set.
findings.impact: A strict-TypeScript consumer following MxIconButton.d.ts cannot pass `disabled` (type error) even though the runtime supports it; a consumer following the prompt.md's guidance would incorrectly conclude disabling is unsupported and build an unnecessary conditional-omission workaround instead of using the existing (if undocumented) prop.
findings.recommendation: Either delete the dead `disabled` handling from MxIconButton.jsx to match the documented "omit instead of disable" guidance, or add `disabled?: boolean` to the d.ts and update the prompt.md States section to describe it as a real state.

findings.title: MxCard's accessible-name prop (ariaLabel) is used in the implementation but never declared in its d.ts or documented in its prompt.md
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/surfaces/MxCard.d.ts:7-19 — MxCardProps has no `ariaLabel` field.
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/surfaces/MxCard.jsx:4,13-15 — `function MxCard({ ..., onClick, style, ariaLabel })` ... when `actionable` (onClick present), wires `'aria-label': ariaLabel` onto the `role="button"` element; MxCard.prompt.md's States/Do-Don't sections (lines 14,18) describe `interactive` + `role="button"` but never mention supplying an accessible name.
findings.description: When an MxCard is made interactive (role="button", tabIndex 0) its accessible name depends entirely on an undocumented, untyped `ariaLabel` prop; if the card's children are non-text content (icons, images, complex layout with no direct text node) the card ships with role="button" and no accessible name unless the caller happens to know about this undeclared prop.
findings.impact: Interactive MxCards built by following only the published contract (d.ts + prompt.md) may end up as unlabeled tap targets for assistive technology whenever the visible content isn't a simple text string, because the mechanism to fix that isn't part of the documented API.
findings.recommendation: Add `ariaLabel?: string` to MxCard.d.ts and document in MxCard.prompt.md's accessibility guidance when an interactive card must supply it (i.e., whenever its children don't resolve to a sufficient accessible name on their own).

findings.title: MxList has no .d.ts and no .prompt.md, unlike every sibling component in navigation/ and surfaces/
findings.evidenceA: Directory listing of docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/surfaces/ shows MxCard, MxContextualAppBar, MxIconTile, MxScaffold, MxSectionHeader each ship a .d.ts + .jsx + .prompt.md triad, while MxList has only MxList.jsx (confirmed via Glob/find over the surfaces/ directory — no MxList.d.ts or MxList.prompt.md exists anywhere in the design system).
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/surfaces/MxList.jsx:1-23 — the file's own header comment ("the ONE vertical list wrapper for ANY stack of cards... Always wrap a card list in this") describes it as load-bearing/mandatory, yet it carries no formal prop typing and no consumer-facing contract (states, accessibility, content limits, do/don't) at all.
findings.description: Every other frozen component in this review group documents its props via .d.ts and its usage/accessibility/i18n contract via .prompt.md. MxList — despite being described as the mandatory wrapper for every card list in the app — has neither, so its `gap` prop's type/default, any accessibility semantics (e.g. whether it should carry `role="list"`), and its states/i18n/RTL behavior are entirely unspecified.
findings.impact: Implementers have no authoritative prop contract or accessibility guidance for a component the kit itself calls mandatory for all list content; behavior not covered by the one-line source comment (e.g. RTL, empty state, list semantics for AT) is left to guesswork.
findings.recommendation: Add MxList.d.ts (props: children, node, gap) and MxList.prompt.md (states/content-limits/accessibility/RTL, matching the format used by every sibling component) to close the parity gap.

findings.title: surfaces.card.html gallery subtitle advertises "AppBar" but never imports or demos MxContextualAppBar
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/surfaces/surfaces.card.html:1 — `<!-- @dsCard group="Components" viewport="700x360" name="Surfaces" subtitle="Card · SectionHeader · IconTile · AppBar" -->`
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/surfaces/surfaces.card.html:19-24 — only `MxCard.jsx`, `MxSectionHeader.jsx`, `MxIconTile.jsx`, and `../core/MxBadge.jsx` are loaded via `text/babel-src`; `MxContextualAppBar.jsx` is never imported, and the `Demo()` function (lines 37-58) never renders an `MxContextualAppBar`.
findings.description: The gallery card's own subtitle promises four things are demoed ("Card · SectionHeader · IconTile · AppBar") but only three of the four component types are actually present in the demo; MxContextualAppBar is entirely absent from this showcase page.
findings.impact: Low — a documentation/gallery accuracy issue only; a reader scanning the surfaces gallery card would expect to see the app bar showcased there and won't find it, adding minor friction when cross-referencing components.
findings.recommendation: Either add an MxContextualAppBar demo block to surfaces.card.html or drop "AppBar" from the subtitle.

findings.title: MxContextualAppBar's "avatar: Root variant only" constraint is documented but not enforced by the implementation
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/surfaces/MxContextualAppBar.d.ts:27 — "Avatar action (MxAvatar). Counts as one of the two allowed actions. Root variant only."
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/surfaces/MxContextualAppBar.jsx:86-93 — the right-cluster render (`actions`, `notification`, `avatar`) is identical for every variant; nothing in the component gates `avatar` to `variant === 'root'`, so passing an `avatar` prop on a `nested`/`search`/`selection`/`modal` bar renders it anyway.
findings.description: This is a docs-only constraint with no runtime guard, consistent with several other unenforced Do/Don't rules across the kit, but worth noting since it's stated as a hard variant restriction ("Root variant only") rather than a soft guideline.
findings.impact: Minor — a misuse (avatar on a non-root bar) would render visually rather than being caught, so the constraint depends entirely on implementer discipline/code review.
findings.recommendation: Low priority: consider a dev-time console warning when `avatar` is passed alongside a non-root variant, or soften the d.ts wording to reflect that it's a guideline rather than an enforced restriction.

findings.title: Same 10 checklist items are simultaneously 'blocking P1 PARTIAL' and 'ACCEPTED/out-of-scope' with no reconciliation
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/mobile-design-kit-audit-v5/issue-register.md:39-48 — e.g. line 39: 'ISS-KIT-32-01 | KIT-32-01 | Thiếu tablet, Web expanded và compact-height evidence. | `P1` | `PARTIAL` | Breakpoint contract added; boundary evidence open | Design System + Flutter UI | Before Tier-1 sign-off'; identical pattern for ISS-KIT-32-02..05 (lines 40-43), ISS-KIT-34-06 (line 44), ISS-KIT-35-06 (line 45), ISS-KIT-36-02/03 (lines 46-47), ISS-KIT-11-05 (line 48). The register itself states it was 're-reviewed 2026-07-18 cho Flutter Web + Android Tier 1' (issue-register.md:3).
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/mobile-design-kit-audit-v5/KIT-32-responsive-va-adaptive-layout.md:49-53 — 'KIT-32-01 | SCOPE.md: tablet/large-screen out of scope (phone-portrait kit) | ACCEPTED'; same pattern for KIT-32-02..05. Also KIT-34-safe-area-va-system-ui.md:51, KIT-35-keyboard-va-input-environment.md:52, KIT-36-orientation-va-device-conditions.md:50-51, KIT-11-layout-va-grid-baseline.md:50 all resolve their matching item as ACCEPTED/out-of-scope via SCOPE.md.
findings.description: Every single one of the register's 10 PARTIAL-status P1 issues has a corresponding row in its own KIT-XX.md Evidence Log where the exact same checklist item was resolved as ACCEPTED via SCOPE.md declaring the underlying capability (tablet, Web-expanded layout, landscape orientation, hardware keyboard, rotation state-preservation) explicitly out of scope. These are mutually exclusive claims: 'out of scope, non-blocking' vs 'in scope, evidence missing, blocks Tier-1 sign-off'. No document reconciles them, and both are presented as current/authoritative within the same audit revision (2026-07-18).
findings.impact: The stated release gate ('BLOCKED, 13 open P1s') is unverifiable as stated: if SCOPE.md's out-of-scope declarations are correct, 10 of the 13 blocking P1s should not exist and the real blocking count is 3 (the ISS-REB-* rows); if issue-register.md's Tier-1 framing is correct, then 10 KIT-XX.md files wrongly closed real gaps as ACCEPTED/scope-exclusions. A Product Owner / Design System owner cannot currently tell which the project actually committed to for the Flutter Web + Android Tier-1 target.
findings.recommendation: Product Owner / Design System owner must decide, once, whether tablet/Web-expanded layout, landscape orientation, and hardware-keyboard input are in scope for the Flutter Web + Android Tier-1 release. Then update either SCOPE.md (if truly out of scope, downgrade the 10 issue-register rows from PARTIAL/P1 to ACCEPTED and recompute the release gate) or the KIT-11/32/34/35/36 Evidence Logs (if in scope, revert the ACCEPTED entries and keep them tracked as genuinely open PARTIAL work).
findings.acceptanceCriteria: issue-register.md and the corresponding KIT-XX.md Evidence Log agree on status/resolution for all 10 items; audit-summary.md's release-gate P1 count and BLOCKED/PASS conclusion is recomputed to match.

findings.title: A dozen 'FIXED' resolutions (including one P0 accessibility issue) cite screenshots/scripts that do not exist anywhere in the repository
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/mobile-design-kit-audit-v5/issue-register.md:25 — 'ISS-KIT-42-02 | KIT-42-02 | ... | `P0` | `FIXED` | tool/a11y/keyboard-walkthrough.mjs + focus-order.*.json (9 stops, primary CTA reachable, no trap, reversible) + axe-report.json (0 serious role/name/aria); guidelines/keyboard-focus-order.md'. Also line 28 (ISS-KIT-21-06, P1, cites 'shoot.mjs' + 'evidence/200pct-dialog-fixed--*.png'), line 56 (ISS-KIT-08-06, P1, cites 'tool/ui_kit_shots/contrast.mjs'), line 34 (ISS-KIT-41-06, P1, cites 'tool/ui_kit_shots/rtl-stress.mjs' + 'evidence/rtl-combined--*.png'), and 8 more FIXED rows (ISS-KIT-25-04, ISS-KIT-35-01, ISS-KIT-35-04, ISS-KIT-37-02, ISS-KIT-42-05, ISS-KIT-09-04, ISS-KIT-09-05, ISS-KIT-11-01) all citing shots/*.png, evidence/*.png, or tool/**/*.mjs paths as their fix evidence.
findings.evidenceB: Filesystem verification (docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/): find . -iname '*.mjs' and find . -iname '*.png' under this tree return nothing under any tool/ or evidence/ path; ui_kits/memox-app/shots/ contains exactly one file, INDEX.md — a manifest of intended filenames whose own header reads 'AUTOGENERATED from tool/ui_kit_shots/registry.mjs... The images are rendered by tool/ui_kit_shots/shoot.mjs' — but neither tool/ui_kit_shots/ nor tool/a11y/ exist anywhere in the repository (repo-wide find . -iname '*.mjs' only turns up the unrelated tool/verify/run.mjs docs-link checker). No evidence/ directory exists at all.
findings.description: The same non-existent tool/evidence problem that issue-register.md itself already documents as ISS-REB-TOOL-01 for KIT-48-03/04 is in fact pervasive across the whole 122-row register: 12 FIXED rows (one of them the P0 keyboard-accessibility issue ISS-KIT-42-02) rest their remediation claim on scripts and PNG/JSON artifacts that are absent from the filesystem. This is documentation drift between the audit's claimed evidence and the actual repository contents.
findings.impact: The release gate's 'Không còn P0' (0 open P0) claim depends on ISS-KIT-42-02 genuinely being fixed, but its sole evidence (a keyboard-walkthrough script + focus-order/axe JSON reports) cannot be reproduced or inspected because it does not exist. The same applies to 7 other P1 FIXED issues and 3 P2 FIXED issues. If evidence cannot be reproduced, these issues cannot be considered verifiably closed, meaning the true open-defect count for this workstream is larger than the stated 0 P0 / 13 P1.
findings.recommendation: Either commit the actual tool/ui_kit_shots and tool/a11y scripts plus their generated PNG/JSON outputs to the repository (so evidence is reproducible), or downgrade these 12 FIXED rows back to OPEN/PARTIAL with an honest note that automated evidence generation is unavailable, and re-verify via whatever evidence-generation pipeline is actually runnable in this repo (tool/verify/run.mjs is the only Node script actually present).
findings.acceptanceCriteria: Every FIXED issue-register row cites evidence that exists on disk and can be independently reproduced/inspected in this repository; ISS-KIT-42-02 specifically has a real, committed keyboard-walkthrough artifact before being counted toward 'P0: 0 open'.

findings.title: This workstream's own explicit 'Design release BLOCKED' status contradicts any 'APPROVED WITH CHANGES APPLIED' framing elsewhere
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/mobile-design-kit-audit-v5/audit-summary.md:6 — 'Design release **BLOCKED** cho tới khi P1 bằng 0.' and line 60 — '[ ] Không còn P1. Hiện còn 13 P1 OPEN/PARTIAL trong issue-register.md.'; issue-register.md:16 — 'Còn mở (OPEN/PARTIAL) ảnh hưởng gate: P0 0 · P1 13. Release status: BLOCKED.'
findings.description: The design-kit-audit-v5 corpus is unambiguous and consistent within itself: the design system release is explicitly BLOCKED, with 13 named open P1 issues and (per finding C4-002) at least one more P0 whose closure is unverifiable. This directly contradicts any characterization of this workstream (e.g., in the separately-flagged, not-independently-verified docs/audits/documentation-remediation-2026-07-18.md, which per task framing claims 'APPROVED WITH CHANGES APPLIED' and nearly everything 'Closed') as approved or complete.
findings.impact: Anyone reading only the higher-level remediation report (not this workstream's own artifacts) would incorrectly conclude the design kit is release-ready, when its own audit trail says otherwise.
findings.recommendation: Any cross-workstream or executive summary document must cite this workstream's real status (BLOCKED, 13 open P1, 1 unverifiable P0) rather than a generic 'approved' rollup; the Lead/Product Owner should reconcile the two documents' verdicts before treating either as final.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: Any top-level remediation/status report references the true BLOCKED status and enumerates the 13 open P1s (or their resolution) instead of declaring the design kit fully approved.

findings.title: issue-register.md's own severity header tally (P1=65, P2=44) does not match its 122 table rows (actual P1=66, P2=43)
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/mobile-design-kit-audit-v5/issue-register.md:15 — 'Severity: P0 6 · P1 65 · P2 44 · P3 7.' Independent row-by-row tally of the 122-row table (script-verified) gives P0=6, P1=66, P2=43, P3=7 (sums to 122 either way).
findings.description: The prose summary line miscounts the table by one row between P1 and P2 (likely one issue is mis-tallied in the header, e.g. a P2 issue counted as P1 or vice versa in the prose only). The release-gate-relevant figure (P1 OPEN/PARTIAL = 13) is unaffected and independently confirmed correct, but the top-line severity distribution is not trustworthy as printed.
findings.impact: Low — does not change the blocking-issue count or release gate, but any reader trusting the printed 'P1 65 / P2 44' totals without recounting will report a wrong total register composition.
findings.recommendation: Regenerate the header counts programmatically from the table (e.g. extend scripts/validate.py to also validate issue-register.md's own summary numbers against its rows) rather than hand-maintaining them.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: issue-register.md's severity header line matches a script-verified tally of its own table.

findings.title: KIT-09 Evidence Log retains stale, superseded PARTIAL rows for KIT-09-04/KIT-09-05 alongside the later FIXED rows for the same IDs
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/mobile-design-kit-audit-v5/KIT-09-typography-system.md:49-53 — rows 49-50 record 'KIT-09-04 | ... | PARTIAL' and 'KIT-09-05 | ... | PARTIAL' (evidence pending CI), then rows 52-53 record the same two IDs again as 'FIXED' with different, fuller evidence.
findings.description: The file's final conclusion (PASS) is correct because the later FIXED rows supersede the PARTIAL ones, but leaving both in the log without marking the earlier row superseded/obsolete makes the Evidence Log read as self-contradictory at a glance.
findings.impact: Purely cosmetic/readability; a reviewer skimming only the PARTIAL rows could wrongly conclude KIT-09 is still open.
findings.recommendation: Mark superseded evidence rows explicitly (e.g. 'SUPERSEDED by row below') or remove them once a later FIXED row exists for the same checklist ID.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: Each checklist ID appears with at most one authoritative resolution row per Evidence Log, or superseded rows are clearly marked as such.

findings.title: OPEN — ISS-REB-UI-01: Tier-1 Flutter Web/Android screenshots missing for core study-flow specs
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/mobile-design-kit-audit-v5/issue-register.md:22 — 'ISS-REB-UI-01 | KIT-44-04 / KIT-48-04 | Deck exclusive, Practice picker, Guess≥5, Recall 20s, SRS read-only và Today recovery đã sửa source/spec nhưng chưa có shot Web/Android hiện hành. | P1 | OPEN'.
findings.description: Core study-flow behaviors (deck-exclusive selection, practice-mode picker, Guess mode ≥5 threshold, Recall mode 20s timer, SRS-state read-only surfacing, Today-list recovery) have had their spec/source rebaselined but no actual Flutter Web/Android light+dark×en/vi screenshots exist confirming the implementation matches the spec.
findings.impact: Cannot confirm the SRS/study-session UI as actually built (once implementation begins) matches the documented contract for these safety/scheduling-relevant behaviors.
findings.recommendation: Produce and attach the Tier-1 Web/Android evidence (light/dark × en/vi) before Tier-1 feature implementation sign-off, per the issue's own target.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: Web/Android screenshot evidence attached for all six named behaviors across light/dark × en/vi; issue status moves to FIXED or ACCEPTED with rationale.

findings.title: OPEN — ISS-REB-I18N-01: en/vi locale not fully externalized, no ARB parity or 200% runtime evidence
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/mobile-design-kit-audit-v5/issue-register.md:23 — 'ISS-REB-I18N-01 | KIT-37-01 / KIT-37-06 | Locale v1 là en/vi nhưng chưa externalize đủ mọi prototype string và chưa có ARB parity + 200% runtime evidence. | P1 | OPEN'.
findings.description: Only a subset of prototype strings are externalized; ARB (Flutter localization) parity between en/vi and runtime 200%-text-scale evidence are both outstanding.
findings.impact: Risk of hardcoded/English-only strings shipping in the Flutter app and of 200%-scale text overflow going unverified for the actual target locales.
findings.recommendation: Complete string externalization and ARB parity check before first vertical-slice release, per the issue's own target; this should tie into the existing lib/l10n/app_en.arb and app_vi.arb files already present in the repo.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: 100% of user-facing prototype copy is keyed/externalized; ARB parity report shows en/vi key sets match; 200% runtime evidence exists for en and vi.

findings.title: OPEN — ISS-REB-TOOL-01: cited npm/parity/a11y verification commands do not exist, so no evidence in this register is reproducible
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/mobile-design-kit-audit-v5/issue-register.md:24 — 'ISS-REB-TOOL-01 | KIT-48-03 / KIT-48-04 | Các lệnh npm/parity/a11y cũ trỏ tới package/tool không tồn tại, nên không tái lập được evidence đã tuyên bố. | P1 | OPEN'.
findings.description: This is the register's own acknowledgment of the problem independently confirmed and generalized in finding C4-002: the verification tooling referenced throughout the audit is not present/runnable in this repository.
findings.impact: Same as C4-002: undermines confidence in every FIXED resolution that depends on this tooling for evidence, and blocks any design sign-off per its own target ('Before any design sign-off').
findings.recommendation: See C4-002's recommendation — either commit working tooling + regenerate evidence, or systematically re-audit and downgrade the FIXED rows that depended on it.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: A canonical, currently-runnable verifier replaces the dangling npm/parity/a11y command references, and release-signoff/coverage-report.md no longer cite unreproducible PASS claims.

findings.title: PARTIAL — ISS-KIT-32-01: missing tablet/Web-expanded/compact-height breakpoint evidence (see also C4-001 scope contradiction)
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/mobile-design-kit-audit-v5/issue-register.md:39 — 'ISS-KIT-32-01 | KIT-32-01 | Thiếu tablet, Web expanded và compact-height evidence. | P1 | PARTIAL | Breakpoint contract added; boundary evidence open | Design System + Flutter UI | Before Tier-1 sign-off'.

findings.impact: Flutter Web layout behavior at wider viewports is unverified.

findings.evidenceA: docs/design/mobile-design-kit-audit-v5/issue-register.md:44 — 'ISS-KIT-34-06 | KIT-34-06 | Chưa có Android landscape/cutout evidence. | P1 | PARTIAL | Safe-region contract added; evidence open | Android UI reviewer'. findings.title: PARTIAL — ISS-KIT-35-06: no Web hardware-keyboard/compact-height evidence
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/mobile-design-kit-audit-v5/issue-register.md:45 — 'ISS-KIT-35-06 | KIT-35-06 | Chưa có Web hardware-keyboard và compact-height evidence. | P1 | PARTIAL | Keyboard/pointer contract added; walkthrough open | Web accessibility reviewer'. Note: KIT-35-keyboard-va-input-environment.md:52 resolves the identical item as ACCEPTED ('landscape + hardware-keyboard out of scope') — same C4-001 contradiction.

findings.evidenceA: docs/design/mobile-design-kit-audit-v5/issue-register.md:47 — 'ISS-KIT-36-03 | KIT-36-03 | Chưa có rotation/resize state-preservation recording. | P1 | PARTIAL | Preservation contract added; recording open | Android UI reviewer'. findings.evidenceA: docs/design/mobile-design-kit-audit-v5/issue-register.md:48 — 'ISS-KIT-11-05 | KIT-11-05 | Chưa có landscape readable-width evidence cho forms/study. | P1 | PARTIAL | Readable width tokens/contract added; evidence open | Design System + Flutter UI'. Note: KIT-11-layout-va-grid-baseline.md:50 resolves the identical item as ACCEPTED ('landscape unsupported; single readable column documented') — same C4-001 contradiction.

findings.title: specs/INDEX.md and index.html gallery registry omit the app-bar screen
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/specs/INDEX.md:9-39 — lists exactly 27 screens (dashboard…settings), Total: 27 screens · 211 states; no app-bar row, no specs/app-bar.md file exists on disk
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/index.html:239 — SCREENS registry includes `{ id: 'app-bar', title: 'App Bar (shared)', ... states: [8 states] }`; docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/shots/INDEX.md:8,356-367 — states its own manifest covers a '219-state matrix (28 screens)' and does list an '## app-bar — App Bar (shared)' section with 8 PNG rows
findings.description: app-bar has a real _features/app-bar/AppBar.jsx, is loaded and stepped through in the interactive gallery (index.html), and has PNG rows in shots/INDEX.md, but has no specs/app-bar.md and is absent from specs/INDEX.md's manifest table, which claims to be generated from the same registry.mjs. The AppBar.jsx header does self-describe it as 'Not an app destination — a component gallery screen', which may be the generator's intentional exclusion criterion, but that rationale is not stated anywhere in specs/INDEX.md, so a reader cannot tell whether this is deliberate or a bug.
findings.impact: A reviewer using specs/INDEX.md as the canonical screen catalog (as instructed to by its own header) will not know app-bar exists as a reference kit or that MxContextualAppBar's full variant set has visual documentation elsewhere; the two 'auto-generated from the same registry' manifests (specs/INDEX.md vs shots/INDEX.md) visibly disagree on the total screen/state count (27/211 vs 28/219), undermining trust in both.
findings.recommendation: Either add app-bar with an explicit 'reference-only, not a spec target' note to specs/INDEX.md, or state explicitly in specs/INDEX.md's header why app-bar is excluded so the two manifests' counts don't silently disagree.

findings.title: dashboard.md documents 4 states (paused/offline/partial/error) that are implemented in Dashboard.jsx but absent from the interactive gallery registry and specs/INDEX.md count
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/specs/dashboard.md:1-46 — 'Rebaselined 2026-07-18...' header, State matrix (contract §6) — 13 states including `paused`, `offline`, `partial`, `error`; text: '`offline` may start only the saved local due queue. `partial` keeps the due action available... `paused` prioritizes Resume... `error` never implies data loss...'
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/index.html:205 — dashboard registry entry lists only 9 states (`loaded, not-studied, goal-met, streak-reset, caught-up, create-sheet, empty, empty-after-onboarding-skip, loading`), no paused/offline/partial/error; docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/dashboard/Dashboard.jsx:113-155 implements `if (state === 'paused')`, `'offline'`, `'partial'`, `'error'` branches; specs/INDEX.md:11 still lists dashboard as 9 states
findings.description: Dashboard.jsx's source code already implements these 4 states with real Mx composition (ActionCallout banners, retry CTAs), and the spec was rebaselined today to document them, but nobody updated the index.html SCREENS array, so the interactive gallery/stepper can never render them, and the shots pipeline (per shots/INDEX.md, which still lists only 9 dashboard states) has no PNGs for them either. dashboard.md's own disclaimer ('require fresh evidence') doesn't mention that they aren't even wired into the review tooling yet.
findings.impact: A designer/reviewer opening index.html to sign off the dashboard screen will never see the paused/offline/partial/error states even though they're documented as part of the contract; specs/INDEX.md's state count is silently wrong for this screen.
findings.recommendation: Add the 4 states to index.html's dashboard SCREENS entry and regenerate specs/INDEX.md/shots/INDEX.md (or, if the tooling to do so doesn't exist — see C5-011 — explicitly flag in dashboard.md that the gallery/shots infrastructure is not yet updated, not just that 'fresh evidence' is pending).

findings.title: recall-mode.md documents a `timed-out` state implemented in RecallMode.jsx that is absent from the gallery registry and specs/INDEX.md count
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/specs/recall-mode.md:1-44 — 'Rebaselined 2026-07-18...'; State matrix — 6 states including `timed-out`; 'Timer behaviour' section: 'At zero, reveal the meaning, record `Timeout` exactly once... Do not silently map timeout to Forgot and do not show both grading buttons.'
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/index.html:225 — recall-mode registry entry lists only 5 states (`before-reveal, revealed, forgot, remembered, complete`), no `timed-out`; docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/recall-mode/RecallMode.jsx:2,12-13 implements `const timedOut = state === 'timed-out';`; specs/INDEX.md:24 still lists recall-mode as 5 states
findings.description: Same drift pattern as dashboard (C5-002): the JSX and the rebaselined spec agree on a new state (`timed-out`, part of the documented core 20-second Recall-stage timeout contract), but index.html's registry and specs/INDEX.md were not updated, so it is unreviewable in the gallery and has no shots.
findings.impact: Recall is stage 4 of the core 5-stage new-learning study session; its timeout terminal state (a distinct, once-only 'Timeout' answer) is a behaviorally important edge case that currently has no way to be visually reviewed or evidenced through the kit's own tooling.
findings.recommendation: Add `timed-out` to index.html's recall-mode SCREENS entry and refresh specs/INDEX.md's count; capture the corresponding shots once the registry is updated.

findings.title: _shared/DeckContentChoice is cataloged as a live composite for a screen and spec that don't exist
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_shared/README.md:15 — `| DeckContentChoice (window.DeckContentChoice) | ../specs/deck-content-choice.md | deck-content-choice screen |`
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_shared/DeckContentChoice.jsx:1-6,54,102 — 'This choice exists only while creating an Empty Deck; established Leaf/Parent kinds cannot be converted by this screen.' No `_features/deck-content-choice/` directory exists, no `specs/deck-content-choice.md` exists, no `deck-content-choice` entry exists in index.html's SCREENS registry, and a repo-wide grep found zero JSX consumers of `window.DeckContentChoice` outside its own definition (compare to index.html:212-217's comment on empty-deck: 'Content-first (§11): the cards-vs-nested choice lives HERE, after creation... Creates an EMPTY deck; one primary CTA')
findings.description: DeckContentChoice.jsx is still loaded by index.html (line 114) as dead weight and is documented in the _shared catalog as a used, spec-backed composite, but it has zero real consumers — the create-deck flow it implements (name + cards-vs-subdecks radio choice at creation time) appears to have been superseded by the current create-deck-firstrun/create-deck-dialog + empty-deck flow (which always creates an empty deck first, then asks cards-vs-nested afterward in empty-deck). This also violates the same README's own admission rule ('used by ≥ 2 screens... used by exactly one screen → it belongs in that screen's _features/<screen>/components/') since it is used by zero screens.
findings.impact: An implementer skimming _shared/README.md would believe there is a 'deck-content-choice' screen and go looking for its spec, wasting time; conversely, a cleanup pass might miss that this file is genuinely stale scaffold debris from an earlier design iteration and either keep shipping it or delete a still-referenced file without checking.
findings.recommendation: Either delete DeckContentChoice.jsx + its index.html script tag as superseded debris, or if it's intentionally retained as a reference/rejected-alternative, remove it from the 'Current members' table (which is for active shared composites) and say explicitly it is retired, mirroring how subdeck-list's retirement is documented in index.html's comment.

findings.title: _shared/README.md's 'Current members' table omits two real, multi-screen shared composites (StudyPromptCard, DeckPlaySheet)
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_shared/README.md:9-22 — the 'Current members' table lists 12 composites (Breadcrumb, ConfirmDialog, StatusCardRow, DeckCard, DeckContentChoice, DeckActionsSheet, DeckMoveSheet, DeckResetConfirmDialog, DeckDeleteConfirmDialog, ActionCallout, SelectSheet, ProfileCard) — no StudyPromptCard or DeckPlaySheet row
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_shared/StudyPromptCard.jsx is `window.StudyPromptCard`, consumed by `_features/recall-mode/RecallMode.jsx:41`, `_features/guess-mode/GuessMode.jsx:46`, and `_features/review-mode/ReviewMode.jsx:59` (3 screens); `_shared/DeckPlaySheet.jsx` is `window.DeckPlaySheet`, loaded by index.html:109 and consumed by `_features/subdeck-list/SubdeckList.jsx:163`
findings.description: Both files live in _shared/, are loaded by index.html after kit-helpers and before feature entries (matching the file-shape convention the README itself defines), and StudyPromptCard clearly satisfies the README's own admission rule ('used by ≥ 2 screens') — yet neither appears in the catalog table whose stated purpose is to prevent this exact kind of undocumented duplication/drift.
findings.impact: A future contributor building a 4th study-mode screen (or a Flutter port task list derived from this catalog) would not discover StudyPromptCard exists and could re-implement the term-prompt-card pattern locally, defeating the DRY goal the _shared/ folder exists for.
findings.recommendation: Add StudyPromptCard and DeckPlaySheet rows to the _shared/README.md catalog table with their consumer lists, matching the existing entries' format.

findings.title: DeckPlaySheet's header comment overclaims usage by Library and Flashcard List
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_shared/DeckPlaySheet.jsx:1-5 — 'opened from a deck / subdeck "Study" (bolt) action on any deck surface (Library, Subdeck List, Flashcard List).'
findings.evidenceB: repo-wide grep for `DeckPlaySheet` finds exactly one JSX consumer: `_features/subdeck-list/SubdeckList.jsx:163`; `_features/library/Library.jsx` and `_features/flashcard-list/FlashcardList.jsx` contain no reference to `window.DeckPlaySheet`
findings.description: The doc-comment describes a 3-surface integration; the actual wired integration is 1 direct consumer (reachable via Library's nested/library-embeds-subdeck-list mechanism, but not from Flashcard List at all).
findings.impact: Minor — could mislead someone auditing where the 'Study' chooser actually appears in the current build versus where it's aspirationally planned to appear.
findings.recommendation: Narrow the comment to what's actually wired today, or mark Library/Flashcard List integration as planned/TODO if intended.

findings.title: SelectSheet's documented 'library (SortSheet)' consumer does not exist in Library's source — the filter/sort sheet is hand-rolled instead
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_shared/SelectSheet.md:5-6,18-19,37 and _shared/SelectSheet.d.ts:29 both state SelectSheet is 'the pattern that mode-picker (ScopeSheet), library (SortSheet) and settings (ValuePickerSheet) previously spelled out row-by-row', with an explicit library-only usage example ('library: rendered inside the screen's existing overlay → no Scrim here... <window.SelectSheet title="Sort by"...'); _shared/README.md:21 lists the same claim
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/library/Library.jsx:177-198 — the `filter-sheet` state ('LIB-10 filter/sort sheet') is implemented as a raw `<Sheet title="Sort & filter" node="library/filter-sheet">` containing hand-written `MenuItem` rows via a local `item(icon,label,s,n)` helper; a repo-wide grep of `_features/library/*.jsx` finds zero references to `window.SelectSheet`
findings.description: Three separate doc artifacts (the .d.ts type contract, the .md usage guide, and the _shared/README catalog) all describe and give a code example for a Library/SortSheet integration with SelectSheet that the actual Library.jsx source does not implement — it duplicates the exact 'title + rows + selected check' shape SelectSheet exists to consolidate, contradicting the K.4 'one shape per concept' principle cited elsewhere in the same doc tree (_shared/README.md:45-55).
findings.impact: Someone porting SelectSheet to Flutter using these docs as the contract would build a component expecting a Library caller that doesn't exist, or would falsely believe Library's filter/sort sheet already follows the shared component and skip auditing it for drift from the canonical pattern (e.g. different check/selection semantics, different spacing).
findings.recommendation: Either update Library.jsx's filter-sheet to actually use `window.SelectSheet` (removing the duplicated MenuItem-row logic), or correct SelectSheet.md/.d.ts/_shared/README.md to remove the false library-usage claim and code example.

findings.title: StatusCardRow's actively-used `clampMeaning` prop is missing from its own type contract (.d.ts) and usage doc (.md)
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_shared/StatusCardRow.d.ts:3-20 (`StatusCardRowProps` interface: term, meaning, deck, status, hidden, node, onClick, tightTerm, truncateMeaning — no `clampMeaning`) and _shared/StatusCardRow.md:17-24 ('## Props' section lists the same 9 props, no `clampMeaning`)
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_shared/StatusCardRow.jsx:9-13,24,28-29,46,57-64 implements a documented `clampMeaning` prop (collapsible meaning with a reserved-height 'Show more' toggle) and it is actively used at every card row in `_features/flashcard-list/FlashcardList.jsx:33,152,231` (`<window.StatusCardRow {...c} tightTerm clampMeaning />`)
findings.description: StatusCardRow.d.ts is explicitly positioned as the 'Flutter parity note' contract (per the sibling ProfileCard.d.ts pattern in this same folder) and StatusCardRow.md as its caller-facing usage guide, but both omit a prop that's core to how Flashcard List's card rows behave (uniform card height via a reserved toggle row) — an important a11y/UX behavior (KIT-style 'every card is exactly the same height' contract) that is undiscoverable from the two docs meant to describe this component's full surface.
findings.impact: A Flutter implementer following StatusCardRow.d.ts/.md as the porting spec would miss the collapsible-meaning behavior entirely and ship a StatusCardRow/MxStatusCardRow without the 'Show more' toggle or fixed-height guarantee that Flashcard List relies on for every row.
findings.recommendation: Add `clampMeaning?: boolean | number` to StatusCardRowProps in the .d.ts and document it (with the 'Show more' toggle behavior) in StatusCardRow.md's Props/Rules sections, mirroring the JSX's own inline documentation.

findings.title: deck-settings.md's auto-derived component map omits all 4 _shared composites the screen actually renders
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/specs/deck-settings.md:23-25 — 'Component map (contract §3) — auto-derived from source: `MxButton`, `MxContextualAppBar`, `MxIconButton`, `MxScaffold`' — and the file's own preamble (line 6) calls this table 'the authoritative set of pieces in play'
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/deck-settings/DeckSettings.jsx:12,39-45 — `const { DeckActionsSheet, DeckMoveSheet, DeckResetConfirmDialog, DeckDeleteConfirmDialog } = window;` then renders each of them for the `move`, `reset-confirm`, `delete-confirm`, `leaf-actions`, and default `action-sheet` states (5 of the screen's 6 documented states)
findings.description: The spec-generation tool that produces the 'auto-derived' component map apparently fails to pick up components referenced via a destructured `const { A, B, C, D } = window;` pattern (or fails specifically for these newer _shared composites) — the four omitted components are exactly the ones that render the majority of this screen's states.
findings.impact: Anyone using deck-settings.md's component map as ground truth for what widgets exist on this screen (e.g. to plan the Flutter port's widget tree, or to audit token usage) would miss 4 of the screen's primary building blocks entirely.
findings.recommendation: Fix the (currently repo-absent — see C5-011) generator to also index destructured `window.X` references, or manually correct deck-settings.md's component map until the generator is fixed/exists.

findings.title: flashcard-list.md's component map omits window.ConfirmDialog, which the screen actually renders twice
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/specs/flashcard-list.md:23-25 — component map lists `MxButton, MxCard, MxChip, MxContextualAppBar, MxFab, MxIconButton, MxLink, MxList, MxScaffold, MxSearchDock, window.ActionCallout, window.Breadcrumb, window.EmptyDeck, window.Note, window.StatusCardRow` — no `window.ConfirmDialog`
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/flashcard-list/FlashcardList.jsx:9,188,272 — `const { ..., ConfirmDialog, FormDialog } = window;` then two live usages: `<ConfirmDialog align="center" scrimNode="flashcard-list/bulk-delete-scrim" .../>` (line 188) and `<ConfirmDialog align="center" scrimNode="flashcard-list/delete-scrim" .../>` (line 272), corresponding to the documented `delete-confirm` state
findings.description: Same root-cause pattern as C5-009: a destructured `window.X` composite import is missed by whatever produced this 'auto-derived' component map, even though flashcard-list.md's own header says this spec was hand-'Rebaselined 2026-07-18' (not purely auto-generated), so this omission slipped through even a manual pass.
findings.impact: Same as C5-009 — undermines the reliability of every 'Component map... auto-derived from source' section across the spec set as a source of truth for what's actually on screen.
findings.recommendation: Add `window.ConfirmDialog` to flashcard-list.md's component map; when the specs are next rebaselined, cross-check destructured `= window;` component references, not just inline `window.X` JSX usages.

findings.title: Nearly every spec file references a Node.js build/regeneration pipeline that does not exist anywhere in this Flutter repository
findings.evidenceA: e.g. docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/specs/account-sync.md:3,5,43-44 — '> AUTOGENERATED from tool/ui_kit_shots/registry.mjs — do not hand-edit; run `npm run gen:ui-kit`.' / 'Verify: `node tool/ui_kit_shots/shoot.mjs account-sync` (gated by `npm run verify:ui-kit`).' — this same boilerplate repeats verbatim across most specs/*.md, specs/INDEX.md, shots/INDEX.md:3,7, _features/README.md:5,39 references `tool/parity/contracts/<id>.gen.json` and `tool/preview/kit-build.md`, and i18n/README.md:41 references a 'kit-build/orchestrator step'
findings.evidenceB: Repo-wide check from the actual repo root (/home/user/memox-v6): `tool/` contains only `tool/verify/run.mjs`; there is no `tool/ui_kit_shots/` directory, no `tool/preview/` directory, no `tool/parity/` directory, and no root `package.json` (so no `npm run gen:ui-kit` / `npm run verify:ui-kit` / `npm run export:all` scripts exist to be run) anywhere in the repository
findings.description: This is documentation-vs-repository drift, not a Business-vs-Design conflict: the design-kit docs describe a self-regenerating build/shot/verify pipeline as if it is a working, checked-in tool, but none of that tooling is present in the repo (AGENTS.md's own tool inventory only lists `tool/verify/run.mjs`). Per instructions this is reported as drift without concluding which side is 'correct' — it may be that this tooling lives in a separate design-authoring repo/environment and was intentionally never checked into memox-v6, or it may be that it was planned but never committed.
findings.impact: Every 'requires fresh evidence' / 'do not hand-edit; run npm run gen:ui-kit' instruction in the spec set (including the C5-002/C5-003 fixes recommended above) currently has no way to be executed from within this repository, so any engineer trying to follow the documented workflow to regenerate/verify specs or shots will be blocked immediately.
findings.recommendation: Product Owner / Design System owner should clarify and document where this tooling actually lives (a separate repo? not yet built?) and either commit it, add a pointer to where it lives, or replace the 'AUTOGENERATED... run npm run gen:ui-kit' boilerplate with accurate maintenance instructions for this repo (as flashcard-list.md and dashboard.md's rebaselined headers already do: 'manually maintained until replacement tooling is committed').

findings.title: PATTERNS.md §4 mixes correct and incorrect relative-path depths for 4 of 6 guideline cross-links
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/specs/PATTERNS.md:118-123 — table rows for Localization/i18n, Overlays/navigation, Keyboard/focus, and Component-constraints all cite bare `guidelines/<file>.md` (no `../../../` prefix)
findings.evidenceB: Same table, rows 1-2 correctly cite `../../../guidelines/flutter-adaptive-layout.md` and `../../../SCOPE.md`; verified on disk: relative to PATTERNS.md's actual location (`ui_kits/memox-app/specs/`), the un-prefixed paths resolve to a nonexistent `ui_kits/memox-app/specs/guidelines/` directory, while the prefixed paths correctly resolve to the real `docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/guidelines/` directory (confirmed to exist with all cited files present)
findings.description: 4 of 6 rows in the same table use the wrong relative depth. This was not caught by the automated docs link checker (0 broken links reported) because these are inline-code table cells (`` `guidelines/i18n-localization.md` ``), not real markdown hyperlinks (`[text](url)`), so the link-integrity tool does not parse them as navigable links.
findings.impact: A reader clicking/copy-pasting these paths from a rendered markdown viewer that treats code spans as plain text won't notice, but anyone scripting a path-resolution check against this table, or manually navigating from the specs/ folder, will hit a dead path for 4 of the 6 cross-referenced guideline docs.
findings.recommendation: Normalize all 6 rows to the correct `../../../guidelines/<file>.md` relative depth.

findings.title: i18n/README.md's migration-path step 1 describes loading strings.js/format.js as a future step, but index.html already loads both
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/i18n/README.md:34-42 — 'Migration path (kit → production) 1. Load the modules. Add, in index.html, before the feature scripts: <script ...i18n/strings.js.../><script ...i18n/format.js.../> (Left to the kit-build/orchestrator step...)'
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/index.html:98-100 — `<script type="text/babel-src" src="i18n/strings.js"></script><script type="text/babel-src" src="i18n/format.js"></script>` already present, loaded before `_shared/*` and all feature scripts
findings.description: The README frames module-loading as a pending/future migration step ('left to... the orchestrator'), but it has already happened in index.html as shipped. This is stale prose left over from before the wiring was completed.
findings.impact: Minor confusion only — a reader might think i18n isn't wired into the gallery yet when it already is.
findings.recommendation: Update step 1 to reflect that the modules are already loaded in index.html, and renumber/reframe the remaining steps as 'wire a feature' / 'add a locale' (steps 2-4 are still forward-looking and accurate).

findings.title: i18n/strings.js contains unused catalog keys (cards.count, due.count) written in ICU plural syntax the kit's own t() function cannot parse
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/i18n/strings.js:49-50 — `'cards.count': '{n, plural, one {# card} other {# cards}}'` / `'due.count': '{n, plural, one {# due} other {# due}}'` (full ICU MessageFormat plural syntax)
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/i18n/strings.js:106-121 — `interpolate()` only does a flat regex substitution (`str.replace(/\{(\w+)\}/g, ...)`) with no ICU plural-category parsing, so calling `t('cards.count', fb, {n:3})` would NOT produce '3 cards' — it would fail to match the `\w+` pattern (the token contains commas/spaces/nested braces) and return the raw unparsed template string; a repo-wide grep confirms zero call sites reference the `cards.count` or `due.count` keys anywhere in _features/_shared (count+noun composition is instead correctly routed through format.js's separate `count()` helper per i18n/README.md's own §Migration notes)
findings.description: These two catalog entries are dead code with a latent bug: if a future contributor ever calls `t('cards.count', ..., {n: 3})` expecting ICU-style pluralization (a reasonable assumption given the ICU-looking template), it will render broken/garbled text instead of '3 cards'. The vi-locale entries for the same keys (`'{n} thẻ'`) are, by contrast, correctly written as plain templates (Vietnamese has no grammatical plural), which makes the en-locale ICU-style entries look intentional/functional when they are not.
findings.impact: No current runtime impact (unused), but a plausible future landmine for whoever tries to use these two specific keys as documented ICU-plural entries.
findings.recommendation: Either remove the two unused keys (count+noun composition is already correctly handled via format.js's `count()` helper) or rewrite them as plain `{n} card(s)`-style fallback templates consistent with how `interpolate()` actually works, and add a one-line comment noting `count()` is the correct API for pluralized counts.

findings.title: Top-level README.md's kit-helpers.jsx helper inventory lists 14 of the 20 actually-exported helpers
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/README.md:17-20 — '`kit-helpers.jsx` holds kit-only composites... (`ProgressBar`, `Skeleton`, `EmptyState`, `DeckRow`, `ListRow`, `Stat`, `Scrim`, `Sheet`, `MenuItem`, `Dialog`, `Note`, `SectionLabel`, `Ring`, `ChoiceOption`) and exports them to `window`.'
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/kit-helpers.jsx:348 — `Object.assign(window, { ProgressBar, ProgressHeader, Skeleton, EmptyState, DeckRow, ListRow, Stat, Scrim, Sheet, MenuItem, MenuList, KeyboardInset, Dialog, DialogInput, FormDialog, Snackbar, Note, SectionLabel, Ring, ChoiceOption });` — 20 helpers total, 6 missing from the README list: `ProgressHeader`, `MenuList`, `KeyboardInset`, `DialogInput`, `FormDialog`, `Snackbar`
findings.description: README.md's helper inventory is stale relative to kit-helpers.jsx, most notably omitting `ProgressHeader` (called out elsewhere in this same doc tree, e.g. _shared/README.md's K.4 conventions table, as 'THE component for every progress bar+count') and `Snackbar` (a full status-toast component with its own detailed tonal-token contract in the source).
findings.impact: Low — this is an orientation paragraph, not a contract; but a new contributor skimming only README.md would not know 6 real, documented-elsewhere helpers exist.
findings.recommendation: Update the parenthetical list to match the current `Object.assign(window, {...})` export list, or replace the enumerated list with a pointer to read the file directly to avoid future drift.

findings.title: Export mock omits business-mandated deck/scope selection and Summary UI
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/deck/export-deck.md §4 (lines 44-60): composition template requires, as the FIRST fields on the screen: "Deck\n<Deck name / selected count>                    Change" and a "Summary\n<deck count> decks · <card count> cards" section before the Export CTA. §6 (lines 71-76) also requires: "Chỉ hiển thị options có hiệu lực với format hiện tại. Đổi format không giữ option không tương thích một cách ẩn" (only show options valid for the current format; switching format must not silently keep incompatible options). §10 State matrix (lines 103-108) requires distinct Leaf/Parent-subtree/multi-select/Empty/mixed-Empty scope states and a share-failure state distinct from generation failure.
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/export/Export.jsx lines 49-70 (the `config` default state): renders SectionLabel SCOPE → MxSegmentedControl('This deck'/'Incl. sub-decks') → SectionLabel FORMAT → FormatList → SectionLabel SEPARATOR → chips → a single always-visible 'Include review state' MxSwitch → the Export button. There is no 'Deck <name> · Change' row, no Summary/count section, and the 'Include review state' switch is not conditioned on the selected format. The full state matrix (specs/export.md, 4 states: config/exporting/export-error/done) has no leaf/parent/multi-select/empty-scope/mixed-empty/share-failure states at all.
findings.description: The business spec for Export Deck defines a required scope-identity affordance ('Deck <name/selected count> · Change') and a Summary of affected decks/cards as core parts of the Export screen's composition, plus format-conditional visibility of options and a distinct share-failure lifecycle state. The design kit's Export mock (which the ui_kit_shots spec generator treats as the canonical source of truth for this screen) implements none of these: it has no deck-scope identity/Change control, no Summary, no format-conditional option filtering, and only 4 states total versus the ~11 the business spec's state matrix calls for. This is either a scope reduction that Product has silently accepted (and the business doc is stale) or a design gap that needs to be built out before Flutter implementation — the two documents currently describe different capability sets for the same screen.
findings.impact: A Flutter engineer building from the design kit alone would ship an Export screen that never lets the user see or change what is being exported, never shows a pre-export summary of affected decks/cards, and has no distinct recovery path for share-vs-generation failures — directly contradicting business acceptance criteria in export-deck.md §12 ('Parent scope aggregate đúng và không double-count', 'Generation/share failures có recovery khác nhau').
findings.recommendation: Product Owner must decide: (a) update docs/business/deck/export-deck.md to match the simplified single-deck-only Export mock (dropping scope/Change/Summary/multi-select requirements), or (b) extend the Export.jsx mock and its spec/state matrix to add the Deck/Change row, Summary section, format-conditional options, and the missing scope/share-failure states before Flutter implementation begins.

findings.title: Deck Settings 'rename' implements a single-field dialog where business defines a full-screen multi-field 'Edit Deck' form
findings.evidenceA:
docs/business/deck/edit-deck.md line 18: "| Deck Settings | Rename/Edit metadata | Cùng form |" (same form serves both rename and metadata edit) and §4 (lines 39-58): Archetype 'Settings/Form', full-screen composition "←  Edit deck

Deck name *
[ Korean TOPIK I ]

Description
[ Vocabulary and grammar for TOPIK I ]

Language pair
Korean → Vietnamese   read-only

[ Save ]", with a full lifecycle (§6, dirty-discard confirm §7, 13 states in §9 including saving/failure/success/discard-confirm/concurrent-update).
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/deck-settings/DeckSettings.jsx lines 27-38 (`rename` state): a CENTERED `Dialog` titled "Rename deck" whose entire body is `<DialogInput label="Deck name" value={TITLE} />` — a single field, no description, no language pair display, no dirty/discard guard, no saving/failure lifecycle. The launching menu item itself (docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_shared/DeckActionsSheet.jsx line 11) is literally labeled "Rename deck", and there is no other JSX anywhere in the design kit implementing a full-screen 'Edit deck' form (grep for 'Edit deck'/'EditDeck'/'edit-deck' across all .jsx/.md returns nothing else).
findings.description: The business spec conflates rename and metadata-edit into one full-screen Settings form with a Description field and read-only Language pair display, plus dirty/discard, saving, and failure states. The design kit only ever offers a bare-bones single-field 'Rename deck' dialog reachable from Deck Settings; there is no way in the design kit to edit a deck's description at all, and none of edit-deck.md's required states (dirty/discard-confirm, saving, failure, duplicate validation while renaming) are represented in the deck-settings mock.
findings.impact: If Flutter is built strictly from the design kit, decks will never be able to have their description edited post-creation (a documented capability in edit-deck.md §1/§5), and the rename flow will be missing required duplicate-name/failure/discard-confirm states that the business spec treats as mandatory (§9, §11).
findings.recommendation: Product Owner must decide whether 'Edit Deck' (full form with description) is in scope for this release. If yes, the design kit needs a new full-screen Edit Deck mock (or the existing rename dialog needs to be expanded) before implementation; if the description field is deliberately out of scope, edit-deck.md should be revised to match the simpler rename-only reality and DeckActionsSheet's action label should be reconciled.

findings.title: Icon-only back/options buttons omit the contractually-required ariaLabel in three features, triggering the documented anti-pattern
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/navigation/MxIconButton.d.ts lines 10-15: "Accessible label — REQUIRED. The button has no visible text; a screen reader must not fall back to the raw icon ligature (e.g. \"arrow_back\", \"more_horiz\"). Use a human label..." and MxIconButton.jsx line 10: `aria-label={ariaLabel || icon}` — the implementation itself performs exactly the fallback the contract forbids.
findings.evidenceB: Three call sites in the assigned feature set omit ariaLabel entirely: account-sync/AccountSync.jsx line 49 `<MxIconButton icon="arrow_back" node="account/back" />`; fill-mode/FillMode.jsx lines 21-22 `<MxIconButton icon="arrow_back" node="fill-mode/back" />` and `<MxIconButton icon="more_vert" node="fill-mode/options" />`; export/Export.jsx line 14 `<MxIconButton icon="arrow_back" node="export/back" />`. Contrast with app-bar/AppBar.jsx, dashboard/Dashboard.jsx, deck-settings/DeckSettings.jsx and empty-deck/EmptyDeck.jsx, which correctly supply ariaLabel on every MxIconButton in the same assigned file set.
findings.description: MxIconButton's own type contract explicitly names the icon-ligature fallback as the exact anti-pattern the required ariaLabel prop exists to prevent, yet the account-sync, fill-mode and export screens' back/options buttons hit that fallback, so a screen reader would announce the raw Material Symbols ligature name (e.g. "arrow back" or "more vert") instead of a human label.
findings.impact: Screen-reader users navigating three separate app screens (account-sync back, fill-mode back/options, export back) hear non-human, potentially confusing button names instead of "Back"/"More options", a systemic and repeated violation of the component's own accessibility contract.
findings.recommendation: Add ariaLabel="Back" to the three back buttons and ariaLabel="More options" (or equivalent) to fill-mode's options button, matching the pattern already used correctly elsewhere in the same feature set (e.g. empty-deck/EmptyDeck.jsx line 17, deck-settings/DeckSettings.jsx line 18).

findings.title: dashboard.card.html gallery listing cites three retired components that no longer exist as files
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/dashboard/Dashboard.jsx line 10: "...(TodaySummary/StreakCard/ContinueCard retired)."
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/dashboard/components/dashboard.card.html line 26: "Composites in this folder (8)" and lines 28-35 list Dashboard, ContinueCard, GoalCard, GreetingHeader, OnboardingHero, OnboardingStep, StreakCard, TodaySummary. Glob of the dashboard/components directory shows only GoalCard.{jsx,d.ts}, GreetingHeader.{jsx,d.ts}, OnboardingHero.{jsx,d.ts}, OnboardingStep.{jsx,d.ts} and dashboard.card.html exist — no ContinueCard.jsx, StreakCard.jsx or TodaySummary.jsx files are present anywhere in the directory.
findings.description: The dashboard feature's own gallery/index card (dashboard.card.html) still lists ContinueCard, StreakCard and TodaySummary as if they were live composites in the folder, directly contradicting Dashboard.jsx's own header comment stating they were retired, and contradicting the actual directory contents (they are not present as files at all).
findings.impact: A developer or designer skimming the gallery card to understand what composites back the Dashboard screen would look for three components that do not exist, wasting time and potentially building against a stale mental model of the screen's composition.
findings.recommendation: Update dashboard.card.html to list the actual 5 live composites (Dashboard, GoalCard, GreetingHeader, OnboardingHero, OnboardingStep) and correct the count from "(8)" to "(5)"; this file appears to be generated/maintained by hand alongside the others (export.card.html, fill-mode.card.html, account-sync.card.html all correctly match their directories) so this is a one-off staleness, not a generator bug.

findings.title: "WBS S.22" cited as the deferred-screen justification for account-sync does not exist in the actual WBS document
findings.evidenceA: docs/wbs/memox-v6-development-wbs.md lines 491-497 ("### 14. Account và Sync"): the only WBS IDs for Account/Sync are 14.1 through 14.6 (Auth/security architecture, Sign-in/out/session recovery, Versioned sync/outbox, Conflict resolution, Account deletion, Sync chaos/security gate). A full-document search for the pattern `S.[0-9]+` returns zero matches anywhere in the WBS file — the WBS uses only numeric dotted IDs (e.g. 0.1, 13.1, 14.1), never an "S." prefix.
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/account-sync/components/SignInCard.d.ts line 5: "The account-sync screen is DEFERRED (WBS S.22) — there is no Flutter counterpart yet, recorded as a `deferred-screen` exception." and SyncBlock.d.ts line 12: "...(WBS S.22) — there is no Flutter counterpart yet, recorded as a `deferred-screen` exception." (same non-existent citation repeated in both files; also appears twice more in the shared ProfileCard.d.ts/.md, outside this workstream's scope).
findings.description: Both feature-local type contracts for account-sync justify the screen's deferred/no-Flutter-counterpart status by citing a WBS ID ("S.22") that does not exist anywhere in the project's actual WBS document. The real, closest-matching WBS entries for Account/Sync are 14.1–14.6.
findings.impact: Anyone trying to trace the account-sync deferral back to a concrete planning/dependency item (e.g. to know when the screen becomes in-scope, or to verify the deferred-screen exception is properly tracked) will fail to find WBS "S.22" and cannot confirm the exception is linked to real planning data — the citation is currently unverifiable/broken.
findings.recommendation: Correct the citation in SignInCard.d.ts and SyncBlock.d.ts (and the two occurrences in _shared/ProfileCard.d.ts/.md) to reference the actual WBS item(s) that gate this screen, e.g. WBS 14.1–14.2, or whichever specific item Product intends.

findings.title: GreetingHeader.eyebrow is typed as a required prop but is never supplied by the only caller, per an explicit design decision documented elsewhere
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/dashboard/components/GreetingHeader.d.ts lines 1-8: `eyebrow: string;` (no `?`, i.e. required) described as "Date line above the greeting (\"Saturday · 27 Jun\")."
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/dashboard/Dashboard.jsx line 53: `const greeting = <GreetingHeader title="Good evening, Linh" node="dashboard/greeting" />;` — eyebrow is never passed, and lines 43-46 explain why: "The date lives ONLY here [the app bar] — the greeting below drops its eyebrow so nothing is duplicated (contract §6)."
findings.description: The type contract marks `eyebrow` as required with no optional marker, but the component's sole real usage in the kit deliberately never supplies it (by explicit design decision, to avoid duplicating the date already shown in the app bar). The .d.ts does not mention this exception at all, so a reader of the type contract alone would conclude every caller must supply an eyebrow date string.
findings.impact: A Flutter implementer following the .d.ts strictly (rather than reading Dashboard.jsx's inline comment) could either wrongly treat eyebrow as mandatory and duplicate the date, or file a false type-mismatch bug against the actual (intentionally eyebrow-less) usage.
findings.recommendation: Mark `eyebrow` optional (`eyebrow?: string`) in GreetingHeader.d.ts and add a short note referencing the app-bar/greeting date-dedup rule, so the contract and the only real usage agree.

findings.title: AppBar.jsx 'modal' variant demonstrates a plain-text Save action that contradicts the design system's own canonical modal-form pattern and the actions-prop contract
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/components/surfaces/MxContextualAppBar.d.ts line 23: "Right-side actions (icon buttons). MAX 2 directly visible; a 3rd+ goes to overflow." — the contract describes `actions` as icon buttons and its accessibility section (lines 38-45) only speaks to icon-only aria-labels, with no provision for a plain text CTA. The one real screen in the kit that uses `variant="modal"`, flashcard-editor/FlashcardEditor.jsx lines 141-145, deliberately leaves the app-bar actions slot empty (`actions={<span aria-hidden="true" />}`) with the comment "Save moved to the sticky bottom bar".
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/app-bar/AppBar.jsx lines 82-86: the `modal` state renders `actions={Save}` — a bare, non-interactive `` with no button role, no tabIndex, no touch-target sizing, and no aria-label, directly inside the actions slot the contract calls "icon buttons".
findings.description: The app-bar reference/gallery screen shows a modal-appbar composition ("Save" as a top-right text action) that the one real screen using this variant elsewhere in the same design system explicitly does NOT use (Save lives in a sticky bottom SaveBar there instead), and that has no accessible/interactive semantics of its own.
findings.impact: A Flutter developer treating app-bar's gallery as a literal reference for 'how to add a modal Save action' would build an inaccessible, non-tappable label instead of following the working flashcard-editor pattern, and would violate the accessibility guidance implicit in the actions-prop contract (icon buttons with aria-labels).
findings.recommendation: Either update AppBar.jsx's modal state to mirror the canonical flashcard-editor pattern (empty actions slot, Save in a sticky bottom bar) or, if a top-bar text Save action is meant to be a legitimate alternate pattern, wrap it as a real button/MxButton with proper touch target and extend MxContextualAppBar.d.ts's contract to document text-action accessibility requirements.

findings.title: GoalCard.d.ts inaccurately describes its own hardcoding, including a 'words' value that does not exist in the component
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/dashboard/components/GoalCard.d.ts lines 4-8: "The kit hardcodes the minutes/words values; Flutter parameterizes them (`goal` / `minutes` / `words`) — recorded as fixture-parameterized exceptions."
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/dashboard/components/GoalCard.jsx line 6: `function GoalCard({ pct, met, minutes = 14, goal = 20 })` — `minutes` is already an accepted, dynamically-threaded prop (not hardcoded) — and Dashboard.jsx line 208 `<GoalCard pct={goalPct} met={met} minutes={idle ? 0 : 14} />` already passes it dynamically per state; no `words` prop or value appears anywhere in GoalCard.jsx.
findings.description: The type contract's prose claims `minutes` is hardcoded in the kit (implying Flutter must newly parameterize it), but the JSX already accepts and varies `minutes`; it also references a `words` value that has no presence in this component at all (word-count is a separate Stat metric in Dashboard.jsx, unrelated to GoalCard).
findings.impact: Low — mostly a documentation-accuracy nit that could cause a reader to either re-implement already-working parameterization or hunt for a non-existent `words` concept inside GoalCard specifically.
findings.recommendation: Correct the .d.ts comment to state that `minutes` is already parameterized in the kit (with a default) and remove the stray reference to `words`, which belongs to a different component.

findings.title: GoalCard's 'goal met' copy hardcodes '20 of 20' instead of templating with the goal variable
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/dashboard/components/GoalCard.jsx line 22: `{met ? '20 of 20 minutes · goal complete' : ...}` — hardcoded literal "20 of 20", while the same component computes `left = Math.max(0, goal - minutes)` (line 7) and templates the non-met branch with `${minutes} of ${goal} minutes`.
findings.evidenceB: n/a (single-file internal inconsistency; the met branch simply never references the `goal`/`minutes` variables it already has in scope)
findings.description: The non-met branch correctly templates its copy from the `goal`/`minutes` props/defaults, but the met branch hardcodes '20 of 20' — which only happens to be correct because `goal` defaults to 20 and is never overridden by the only caller (Dashboard.jsx).
findings.impact: If a Flutter implementation follows this pattern literally for a caller with a non-default daily goal (e.g. 30 minutes), the 'met' message would incorrectly read '20 of 20 minutes' instead of '30 of 30 minutes'.
findings.recommendation: Change the met-branch string to `${goal} of ${goal} minutes · goal complete` to match the templating already used in the non-met branch.

findings.title: Generated spec handoff notes disagree on the touch-target minimum (44×44 vs 48×48) versus the actual --memox-touch-min token
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/specs/dashboard.md line 51: "touch targets ≥ 48×48" — matches the actual token, tokens/spacing.css line 47: `--memox-touch-min: 48px;`
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/specs/account-sync.md line 43, add-card-target.md line 37, create-deck-dialog.md line 49, create-deck-firstrun.md line 48, deck-settings.md line 41, empty-deck.md line 39, export.md line 39, and fill-mode.md line 41 all say instead: "touch targets ≥ 44×44".
findings.description: Eight of the nine spec files in this workstream state a different touch-target minimum (44×44, matching the business doc's minimum bar rather than the actual design-system token), while only dashboard.md (rebaselined 2026-07-18 per its header) states the 48×48 value that matches --memox-touch-min. Since 48px ≥ 44px this is not a functional violation, just an inconsistent generated figure.
findings.impact: Low — no functional conflict since 48px already exceeds the 44px business minimum, but the specs disagree with each other and with the actual token value, which could confuse anyone auditing touch targets against the stated figure.
findings.recommendation: Regenerate/update the boilerplate handoff-notes sentence in the affected spec files to read "≥ 48×48" to match the actual --memox-touch-min token and dashboard.md's already-corrected wording.

findings.title: CreateDeckDialog's language-pair selector lacks the interactive-affordance styling/role used by the equivalent selector in CreateDeckFirstRun and AddCardTarget
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/create-deck-firstrun/CreateDeckFirstRun.jsx line 37 (`SelectRow`): `className={disabled ? '' : 'card--interactive'}` on the pair-picker row; docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/add-card-target/AddCardTarget.jsx line 14 (`DeckRow`): `role="option" ... className={disabled ? '' : 'card--interactive'}`.
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/create-deck-dialog/CreateDeckDialog.jsx lines 22-33 (`DField`, used for the pair selector at line 76): plain `<div data-mx-node={node} style={{...}}>` with no `card--interactive` class, no role, and no tabIndex, despite serving the same 'tap to open a picker' function as the other two rows.
findings.description: Two other tappable pseudo-select rows in the same assigned feature set (CreateDeckFirstRun's language SelectRow, AddCardTarget's DeckRow) mark themselves as interactive via the `card--interactive` class (and one adds `role="option"`), but CreateDeckDialog's structurally identical pair-selector field does not, despite performing the same function (opening the pair picker, as seen in the `root-multi-pair` state).
findings.impact: Minor visual/semantic inconsistency — the pair selector in the Create Deck dialog would not get the same hover/press affordance styling as equivalent controls elsewhere in the kit, and lacks any interactive-role signal for a future Flutter/semantics implementation to key off.
findings.recommendation: Add the `card--interactive` class (and ideally a role, e.g. role="button" or role="combobox") to DField's wrapper when it represents the tappable pair selector, matching the pattern already used in CreateDeckFirstRun.jsx and AddCardTarget.jsx.

findings.title: deck-settings.md declares Primary CTA "Save" though the screen's default entry state has no CTA at all
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/specs/deck-settings.md line 11: "Primary CTA: **Save**" and line 15: "Archetype (contract §2): Settings".
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/deck-settings/DeckSettings.jsx lines 44-45: the default `action-sheet` state renders `<DeckActionsSheet title={TITLE} />`, an action-sheet menu of five co-equal peer actions (Rename/Move/Export/Reset/Delete, per _shared/DeckActionsSheet.jsx lines 11-15) with no CTA at all; "Save" only appears two states deep, inside the `rename` sub-dialog.
