findings.evidenceA: docs/decision-tables/README.md:19 — `Given/When/Then dùng domain values; không dùng widget class hoặc database implementation detail.` states the general contract for all tables in the catalog.
findings.evidenceB: docs/decision-tables/srs-8-box-v1.md:6 uses headers `| ID | Current/Given | Event | Next/Then | dueAt |` and docs/decision-tables/backup-sync-integrity.md:4 uses `| ID | Context | Condition | Result |` — neither uses literal Given/When/Then column names (unlike study-session-types.md, study-session-content-changes.md, match-outcomes.md and fill-answer-normalization.md, which all use `Given | When | Then`).
findings.description: Semantically the two outlier tables still encode a given/trigger/outcome shape, so this is not a functional defect, just a documented-convention deviation.
findings.impact: Minor readability/consistency issue only.
findings.recommendation: Normalize SRS8-v1 and DATA-MERGE-v1 headers to Given/When/Then (or explicitly note in README.md that these two tables use a domain-specific header naming for readability reasons).

findings.title: 'failed set' (intra-session mastery rounds) and 'missed Card set' (inter-session relearn) are used without an explicit equivalence/distinction statement
findings.evidenceA: docs/wbs/memox-v6-development-wbs.md:397 — `5.6.11 CP | Mastery rounds and relearn | XL | 5.6.5–5.6.10 | Failed set deduped, unlimited retry rounds, separate persistence retry/relearn namespaces.`
findings.evidenceB: docs/decision-tables/study-session-types.md:11 — `ST-TYPE-007 | Finalized session có missed Card set | Start relearn | Snapshot missed set; không mutate source session`
findings.description: 5.6.11 describes an intra-session concept ('failed set' — cards that failed within the current session's mastery rounds, retried until correct) while ST-TYPE-007 describes an inter-session concept ('missed Card set' — the set carried over from a finalized session to seed a brand-new `relearn` session). Both files use 'failed'/'missed' interchangeably-sounding vocabulary for what are stated to be different lifecycle scopes (5.6.11 also says relearn has a 'separate persistence retry/relearn namespace' from mastery-round retries, implying the two sets are deliberately distinct), but neither file states explicitly whether/how a session's terminal 'failed set' becomes the next relearn session's 'missed Card set'.
findings.impact: Low — likely resolved in docs/business/study-session/relearn-cards.md (outside this workstream's assigned files), but as read, a domain/data implementer working only from the WBS + decision tables could reasonably build two disconnected sets rather than one clean handoff.
findings.recommendation: Add one sentence in study-session-types.md or the WBS cross-referencing that a finalized session's mastery-round 'failed set' is exactly what becomes ST-TYPE-007's 'missed Card set' for a subsequent relearn session (or clarify why they differ).

findings.title: Decision table SRS8-v1 omits the Box 7 -> wrong transition despite being titled 'exhaustive' and requiring row-to-test parity
findings.evidenceA: docs/decision-tables/srs-8-box-v1.md:1 title 'Leitner 8 Box exhaustive decision table'; the table enumerates an explicit per-box wrong row for every box except 7 — SRS8-004 (Box 1 wrong, line 10), SRS8-018 (Box 2 wrong, line 24), SRS8-020 (Box 3 wrong, line 26), SRS8-006 (Box 4 wrong, line 12), SRS8-022 (Box 5 wrong, line 28), SRS8-024 (Box 6 wrong, line 30), SRS8-009 (Box 8 wrong, line 15). Box 7 appears only as correct (SRS8-007, line 13). There is NO row for 'Box 7 | wrong -> Box 6 | nowUtc + 60d'.
findings.evidenceB: docs/business/learning-progress/srs-8-box-policy.md:162 'Mỗi row bắt buộc có ít nhất một test và test phải dẫn exact ID'; docs/decision-tables/README.md:14-18 requires every row to map to a test and a coverage checker to fail on gaps. The generic aggregate row SRS8-010 (line 16, 'apply one decrement ... Interval of resulting box') covers sticky-wrong abstractly but provides no explicit ID/test for the Box 7->6 (+60d) interval, which every other box's wrong case receives.
findings.description: Documentation gap: the SRS decision table asserts exhaustiveness and the policy/README mandate a test per transition row, yet the Box 7 -> wrong transition (regress to Box 6, dueAt = nowUtc + 60 days per policy §5/§6) has no dedicated row or ID. Box 7 wrong is the only wrong-case that produces a +60d interval and is a genuine boundary (top scheduled box regressing one step before mastery). The behavior is unambiguously defined by the pure formula in srs-8-box-policy.md:89 and :111, so this is not a correctness conflict, but the executable-contract test coverage for this transition is missing.
findings.impact: When implementers derive property/decision-table tests from srs-8-box-v1.md (per WBS FD-02/FD-06 and 5.4.5), the Box 7->6 transition will lack a required test ID, leaving the highest-interval regression path unverified and weakening the 'exhaustive' guarantee the table advertises.
findings.rootCause: The per-box wrong rows were added (SRS8-017..024 fill in boxes 2,3,5,6) but Box 7 wrong was overlooked; the 'exhaustive' claim and the row-to-test parity contract are therefore not met for that single transition.
findings.recommendation: Add an explicit row, e.g. 'SRS8-029 | Box 7 | wrong | Box 6 | nowUtc + 60d', alongside the existing per-box wrong rows, and reference it from the Progress test suite (WBS 5.4.5). Alternatively, if SRS8-010 is intended to be the sole coverage for all wrong transitions, drop the redundant per-box wrong rows for consistency — but keeping 7 of 8 explicit and omitting one is the defect.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: srs-8-box-v1.md contains an explicit Box 7 wrong -> Box 6 (+60d) row with a stable ID that a test references, or the table's per-box wrong rows are made uniformly generic; the 'exhaustive' claim then holds for all 8 boxes x {correct, wrong}.

findings.title: Stale 'SRS8-001..016' ID range referenced in decision-table header and WBS 0.3 while the table actually spans SRS8-001..028
findings.evidenceA: docs/decision-tables/srs-8-box-v1.md:3 'IDs `SRS8-001..016` preserve the canonical summary IDs in the policy document.' — but the table body defines rows SRS8-001 through SRS8-028 (lines 7-34).
findings.evidenceB: docs/wbs/memox-v6-development-wbs.md:244 (WBS 0.3) '... `leitner-8-box-v1` decision rows SRS8-001..016 agree ...' — same stale upper bound of 016.
findings.description: Documentation drift: two governance-level references still cite the frozen canonical set as SRS8-001..016, whereas the executable table now contains SRS8-001..028. This understates the frozen row set and could cause rows 017-028 to be treated as non-canonical or uncovered by the row-to-test coverage checker described in decision-tables/README.md:16-18.
findings.impact: A reviewer or coverage tool trusting the '..016' bound may not enforce tests for rows 017-028 (which include the majority of the per-box transitions), and the WBS DG-03/0.3 sign-off references an out-of-date row set.
findings.rootCause: The table was expanded from an original 16-row summary to 28 rows (adding SRS8-017..028 for the remaining per-box transitions, deleted-card, practice, and unknown-policy cases) without updating the '..016' range references in the header line and the WBS governance gate.
findings.recommendation: Update srs-8-box-v1.md:3 and WBS 0.3 (line 244) to reference the actual range (SRS8-001..028), or state 'SRS8-001..NNN' generically, so the frozen canonical set matches the table.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: Both the decision-table header and WBS 0.3 cite the same, current ID range that matches the rows actually present in srs-8-box-v1.md.

findings.title: Prior audit report's 'Exhaustive transition table ... Closed' SRS verdict is not fully substantiated
findings.evidenceA: docs/audits/documentation-remediation-2026-07-18.md:37 '| SRS safety | Exhaustive transition table, UTC/due semantics, sticky-wrong, terminal-once/idempotency/transaction/migration | .../srs-8-box-v1.md | Closed |'.
findings.evidenceB: docs/decision-tables/srs-8-box-v1.md:13 (Box 7 appears only as correct, SRS8-007) with no Box 7 wrong row anywhere in lines 7-34 — see finding SRS8-001. The table is therefore not exhaustive as the audit claims.
findings.description: Per the audit-verification mandate, the prior report's 'Closed' claim for 'Exhaustive transition table' is contradicted by the actual content: one of the sixteen box x outcome combinations (Box 7 wrong) is absent. Everything else the row asserts (UTC/due semantics, sticky-wrong, terminal-once, idempotency, transaction, migration) does check out; only the exhaustiveness sub-claim is overstated.
findings.impact: Relying on the prior audit's 'Closed' status would let the Box-7-wrong test-coverage gap ship unfixed.
findings.rootCause: The prior remediation marked SRS transition-table exhaustiveness as Closed without verifying that every box x {correct,wrong} pair has a row; the Box 7 wrong omission was not caught.
findings.recommendation: Treat SRS transition-table exhaustiveness as open until finding SRS8-001 is resolved; then the 'Closed' status is warranted.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: Audit exhaustiveness claim holds only after the Box 7 wrong row is added; audit row updated if it is meant to reflect true state.

findings.title: Overloaded term 'round': design Settings shows 'Words per round: 5' while business defines a graded-mode round as ALL valid cards in the snapshot
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/settings/Settings.jsx:103 'Words per round' sub 'Cards shown each round' value 5; and :69 study-hub sub '5 words/round · shuffle'.
findings.evidenceB: docs/business/study-session/start-study-session.md:91 'Khi bắt đầu mỗi graded mode, roundIndex = 1, currentRoundCardIds nhận toàn bộ Card hợp lệ trong session snapshot và nextRoundFailedCardIds rỗng.' and docs/business/study-session/answer-study-stage.md:91 (same model) — a graded-mode round contains the full valid card set, not a fixed 5.
findings.description: This is a cross-workstream naming ambiguity rather than a hard SRS-box conflict, so it is flagged P3 for the Study-Session/Preferences reviewer rather than as a P0 SRS business-vs-design conflict. If 'words per round: 5' is intended as the new-card introduction limit (surface-due-cards.md:48-49 and start-study-session.md:89 both reference a 'new-card limit'), there is no real disagreement; but the shared word 'round' with two meanings invites misimplementation where UI code chunks a graded mode into fixed groups of 5, contradicting the business 'currentRoundCardIds = all valid cards' contract.
findings.impact: Potential misimplementation of graded-mode round sizing (fixed-5 chunking vs full-set) and reader confusion; does not affect SRS box scheduling itself.
findings.rootCause: The word 'round' is used for two different concepts: the design 'words per round' preference (plausibly a per-session card batch / new-card limit) versus the business 'mastery round' (round 1 = all valid cards, later rounds = deduped failed set).
findings.recommendation: Disambiguate the design copy (e.g., 'New cards per session' / 'Cards per session') or add a business note mapping the 'words per round' preference to the new-card limit, and reserve 'mastery round' for the failed-set retry loop. Escalate to Study-Session/Preferences owner to confirm whether the two are the same knob.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: The design 'words per round' preference is explicitly reconciled to a named business concept (new-card limit vs mastery round), with no ambiguity about whether graded-mode round 1 uses all valid cards or a fixed count.

findings.title: Remove Language Pair: design cascade-deletes all decks/cards; business forbids cascade and blocks on dependency
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/language-pair/remove-language-pair.md §1 (lines 7-9): "Pair có Deck dependency không được xóa trực tiếp. / Không cascade delete Deck/Card. / Remove là destructive explicit confirmation." and §6 acceptance (line 47) "Không có orphan Deck hoặc cascade delete."; master flow (lines 21-23) routes "Has Deck dependencies? -- Có --> Block · list/manage Decks".
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/languages/components/RemoveLanguageDialog.jsx: dialog text "All decks and cards for this pair will be deleted. This can't be undone." with a single "Remove" primary action; and Languages.jsx line 90-92 renders this RemoveLanguageDialog for the `remove` state unconditionally with no dependency-count / blocked branch.
findings.description: Direct contradiction on the destructive semantics of removing a language pair. Business mandates that a pair with dependent decks CANNOT be removed (removal is blocked and the user is routed to manage decks) and that Deck/Card are NEVER cascade-deleted. The design's only removal surface is a confirm dialog whose copy promises to delete all decks and cards, and it has no blocked/dependency state at all.
findings.impact: If the design is implemented as drawn, a single confirm destroys the learner's entire deck/card corpus for that pair, which the business explicitly forbids as data loss. If the business rule is implemented, the design's dialog copy and flow are wrong.
findings.recommendation: Product Owner must decide the canonical Remove Language Pair semantics: (a) block removal when dependent decks exist and require the user to move/delete decks first (business), or (b) allow an explicit cascade delete of decks+cards (design). Then reconcile the losing document.

findings.title: Account sign-in mock pre-commits to Google + email/password providers with live CTAs; cloud-service-gate defers provider and requires CTAs absent/unavailable
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/account/cloud-service-gate.md line 3 "Status: Deferred for v1; mandatory before provider work", line 5 "Tier-1 default: local-only; no cloud provider selected", line 9 "No implementation may infer a provider from a UI mock", line 26 "Until the gate is accepted, cloud CTAs are absent or explicitly labelled unavailable". Provider/auth mechanisms must be recorded in the decision record (line 15) before this work is Ready.
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/account-sync/AccountSync.jsx: EmailSignInForm renders a full email + password form (AuthField lines 35-40) with a primary CTA "Continue with email" (line 41) and an outline CTA "Sign in with Google" (line 43); comment line 30 "Email sign-in form — the password-manager path alongside Google sign-in." The signed-out state (lines 51-57) presents SignInCard as active content with only a soft "This feature is in alpha." caption, not an unavailable/absent CTA.
findings.description: The design mock infers and pre-commits two concrete cloud auth providers (Google OAuth + email/password) and ships them as live primary/secondary CTAs, which is exactly what the cloud-service-gate says no implementation may do until Product/Security record the provider decision. The 'alpha' caption is a hedge but does not make the CTAs 'absent or explicitly labelled unavailable'.
findings.impact: Engineers reading the frozen kit will wire Google + email/password auth, silently making the deferred provider/security decision the gate says must be made deliberately with data-region/encryption/retention analysis first.
findings.recommendation: Product Owner + Security must decide: keep account/sync deferred (then the design must remove or disable the provider-specific CTAs and de-commit Google/email-password), or ratify a provider now via the cloud-service-gate decision record. Do not let the mock be the decision.

findings.title: Deck rename is a single-field dialog in design; business defines a full-screen Edit Deck form (name + description + read-only language pair)
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/deck/edit-deck.md §1 "Deck name bắt buộc; description optional... Language pair hiển thị read-only", entry points (lines 17-20) "Deck Settings | Rename/Edit metadata | Cùng form" all pointing at a "Full-screen Settings form", and §4 composition (lines 45-58) shows Deck name + Description + read-only Language pair fields with a single Save CTA.
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/deck-settings/DeckSettings.jsx lines 27-38: the `rename` state renders a centered Dialog titled "Rename deck" whose entire body is a single DialogInput label "Deck name" (no description field, no read-only language pair) with Cancel/Save.
findings.description: Business defines editing deck metadata as a full-screen form editing name AND description, with the language pair shown read-only; the same doc explicitly says Deck Settings' rename/edit-metadata entry opens that same full form. The design implements only a bare one-field rename dialog, dropping the description field and the read-only pair display.
findings.impact: The design cannot capture or edit deck description at all, and gives no read-only pair context; implementing the design silently drops a documented editable field and a full acceptance surface (duplicate-root vs sibling validation, dirty-guard, etc.).
findings.recommendation: Product Owner must decide whether deck metadata editing is a single-field rename dialog or the full-screen Edit Deck form (name+description+read-only pair). Align deck-settings design and edit-deck.md to one.

findings.title: Card audio: design collapses to auto-generate + play only; business requires Generate / Attach file / Remove as first-class actions
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/flashcard/manage-card-audio.md §1 (line 7) "Flow này sở hữu generate/attach/play/remove audio reference", §4 composition (line 49) "Generate audio    Attach file    Remove", §5 covers an Attach path, and §9 state matrix (line 84) "Attach valid/invalid ... regenerate; remove."
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/flashcard-editor/components/AudioRow.jsx header comment: "The pronunciation control, collapsed from a full-width row into a COMPACT icon button... Pronunciation is auto-generated from the term, so it no longer needs its own section — a tap plays it." MAP only defines auto/ready/generating/error (play + retry); there is no Attach and no Remove action. FlashcardEditor.jsx line 174 wires AudioRow with only status auto/generating.
findings.description: Business owns four audio capabilities (generate, attach a user file, play, remove) with their own state matrix. The design deliberately dropped Attach-file and Remove, reducing audio to auto-generate + play. This is a capability-scope contradiction, not just a layout change.
findings.impact: Learners cannot attach their own pronunciation file or remove a bad/auto-generated one in the design; implementing the kit as frozen silently removes two business-owned actions and their entire failure/validation matrix.
findings.recommendation: Product Owner must decide whether v1 card audio supports Attach-file and Remove (business) or is auto-generate+play only (design). Reconcile manage-card-audio.md and the AudioRow contract accordingly.

findings.title: Additional translations: design supports exactly one extra translation; business requires an ordered, reorderable, multi-item list
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/flashcard/manage-card-translations.md §1 "Additional translation có stable identity và explicit order", §4 composition (lines 42-47) shows a numbered list "[1 ...] [2 ...] Add translation", §5 "Order positions contiguous/unique after Save. Reorder uses accessible move actions...", §9 acceptance "additional entries nonblank/unique/ordered".
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/flashcard-editor/FlashcardEditor.jsx: a single "Add translation" icon button (line 179) and a single `additional-translation` state that renders exactly one Translation field with a Remove action (lines 183-187). There is no list, no ordering, no reorder control, and no way to add a second translation.
findings.description: Business models additional translations as an ordered, reorderable, de-duplicated multi-item collection with stable identity per entry. The design's editor supports adding exactly one extra translation with no order or reorder affordance.
findings.impact: Cards limited to one extra meaning in the design cannot represent the business-mandated multi-translation, ordered set; reorder acceptance criteria are unimplementable against the frozen contract.
findings.recommendation: Product Owner must decide whether cards carry one additional translation (design) or an ordered multi-item list with reorder (business), then align FlashcardEditor and manage-card-translations.md.

findings.title: Duplicate-flashcard resolution: design offers only View-existing / Add-anyway; business requires Edit / Open-existing / Keep-both / Merge with side-by-side comparison
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/flashcard/resolve-duplicate-flashcard.md §2 (lines 18-19) Create-draft decisions "Edit draft, open existing, keep both, merge draft fields"; §4 composition (lines 43-48) shows a side-by-side "New / edited card | Existing card" comparison with "Review the differences before saving."; §5 defines Keep-both and Merge semantics; §8 "Primary content conflicts require user decision."
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/flashcard-editor/components/DupBanner.jsx: a compact warning banner "A card “안녕하세요” already exists in this deck." with exactly two actions — "View existing" (secondary) and "Add anyway" (ghost). No keep-both/merge decisions and no side-by-side comparison of incoming vs existing content.
findings.description: Business defines a rich four-way decision surface (Edit, Open existing, Keep both, Merge) plus mandatory side-by-side content comparison before saving a possible duplicate. The design reduces this to a two-way banner (inspect existing, or force-add), with no merge, no explicit keep-both semantics, and no comparison.
findings.impact: The design cannot express Merge or the explicit audited Keep-both decision, and offers no comparison for the user to make an informed choice, which the business requires to prevent unintended duplicates/merges.
findings.recommendation: Product Owner must decide the duplicate-resolution decision set and whether a comparison view is required for v1; align DupBanner with resolve-duplicate-flashcard.md.

findings.title: Import duplicate handling: design shows one generic 'import anyway?' banner; business requires per-item/bulk Skip / Merge / Import-anyway with incoming-vs-existing comparison
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/content-transfer/resolve-import-duplicates.md §1 "Decision có thể per-item hoặc bulk", "Merge chỉ dùng Flashcard merge contract"; master flow (lines 18-26) branches Skip / Merge / Import anyway; §3 "Incoming/existing content và Deck path phải phân biệt rõ"; §5 state matrix includes "Skip/merge/keep-both/bulk/undo".
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/import/Import.jsx line 113-114: the `dup-warning` state renders a single window.ActionCallout "8 cards already exist — import anyway?" over the preview, then a single "Import 124 cards" button. There is no Skip, no Merge, no per-item/bulk resolution, and no incoming-vs-existing comparison.
findings.description: Business defines import duplicate resolution as a compare/resolution list with per-item or bulk Skip/Merge/Import-anyway decisions and undo, distinguishing incoming vs existing content. The design collapses it to one all-or-nothing 'import anyway' banner.
findings.impact: The design cannot skip or merge specific duplicate rows and offers no comparison; implementing it discards a whole business-owned resolution capability and its acceptance criteria (no candidate overwritten without explicit decision).
findings.recommendation: Product Owner must decide the import-duplicate resolution model for v1 (all-or-nothing vs per-item/bulk Skip/Merge/Import-anyway with comparison); align Import.jsx with resolve-import-duplicates.md.

findings.title: 'Mode settings' screen mismatch: design exposes words-per-round(5)/shuffle/autoplay; business 'Configure Mode Preferences' (enable/disable/reorder modes) has no design surface and 'Words per round: 5' contradicts the round=all-cards model
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/preferences/configure-mode-preferences.md §1-3: the feature configures "mode availability/default order", "Enable/disable/reorder modes", "Choose default", "Mỗi mode có name, short purpose, enabled state và minimum note" with accessible reorder. Separately docs/business/study-mode/README.md line 14: "Round đầu nhận toàn bộ Card của stage" and line 16 "Không giới hạn số mastery round" (a graded round is ALL valid cards, not a fixed N).
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/settings/Settings.jsx `study-mode` state (lines 100-109) titled "Mode settings" contains only: "Words per round" trailing value 5 (line 103, sub "Cards shown each round"), "Shuffle cards" toggle, "Autoplay audio" toggle; and the hub row (line 69) sub "5 words/round · shuffle". There is NO mode enable/disable, no reorder, and no default-mode selection anywhere in Settings.
findings.description: The business capability 'Configure Mode Preferences' (enable/disable/reorder study modes and pick a default) has no corresponding surface in the design's Settings. Instead the design 'Mode settings' screen exposes controls (words-per-round, shuffle toggle, autoplay) that are not owned by configure-mode-preferences.md; and 'Words per round: 5' directly contradicts the business model where a graded round contains ALL valid cards of the stage (only the option COUNT is 5 in Guess, not a per-round word cap). 'Shuffle cards' as an on/off toggle also conflicts with the order-randomization contract that mandates deterministic shuffle always.
findings.impact: Two documents disagree on what the Mode Preferences/Settings screen is: the enable/disable/reorder capability is undeliverable from the design, and a 'words per round = 5' setting, if implemented, would change the core round model (all-cards) that Match/Guess/Recall/Fill and start-study-session depend on.
findings.recommendation: Product Owner must decide (a) whether mode enable/disable/reorder + default is an in-scope v1 capability that needs a design screen, and (b) whether a configurable 'words per round' exists at all — if yes, the business round=all-cards model must be revised via decision table; if no, remove it from the design. Reconcile Settings.jsx, configure-mode-preferences.md and study-mode/README.md.

findings.title: Export design omits the business-mandated Summary review (deck/card counts) and scope-change affordance
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/deck/export-deck.md §4 layout (lines 47-59): "Deck <name/selected count> Change" and "Summary <deck count> decks · <card count> cards" above the Export CTA; §1 "Scope và options được review trước khi tạo file"; §5 covers multi-select and mixed-empty scope; entry points include "Global Settings | Chọn Deck bắt buộc".
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/export/Export.jsx config state (lines 49-69): a two-value scope MxSegmentedControl ("This deck"/"Incl. sub-decks"), format list, separator chips, one include-review-state switch, and the Export button. There is no Summary block showing deck/card counts and no deck-picker 'Change' affordance for arbitrary/multi decks.
findings.description: The design provides a basic Leaf/subtree scope toggle but omits the business-required pre-submit Summary (counts the user reviews before generating) and the 'Change'/deck-selection affordance needed for the Library-selection and Global-Settings entry points. This is an omission (design silent), not a direct contradiction.
findings.impact: Users cannot review the aggregate deck/card counts before exporting, and the arbitrary-deck/multi-select entry points have no surface, weakening the 'review before create file' acceptance criterion.
findings.recommendation: Add the Summary count block and a scope 'Change'/deck-picker to the export design, or have the Product Owner confirm the reduced single-deck-context export is the intended v1 scope and trim export-deck.md accordingly.

findings.title: Graded-mode design state matrices omit business-required canonical states: round-complete, retry-round (as distinct from complete) and Guess invalid-distractor-pool
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/study-mode/README.md 'Canonical state coverage' (lines 75-78) requires: Match "...almost/round-complete/retry-round/complete"; Guess "...wrong/invalid-distractor-pool/round-complete/retry-round/long-text/complete"; Recall "...Remembered; ...round-complete/retry-round/complete". Reinforced by match-terms-and-meanings.md §5 (line 68) and guess-card-meaning.md §1/§5 (invalid-distractor-pool is a required recoverable question error that must render no question and create no Attempt).
findings.evidenceB: Design specs enumerate fewer states: match-mode.md (lines 31-36) playing/selected/correct/wrong/almost/complete; guess-mode.md (lines 29-35) waiting/correct/wrong/long-text/complete; recall-mode.md (lines 30-35) before-reveal/revealed/forgot/remembered/timed-out/complete. The single design `complete` state (e.g. GuessMode.jsx line 32 'Round complete!' + 'Next round') conflates round-complete and retry-round, and there is no `invalid-distractor-pool` branch anywhere in GuessMode.jsx.
findings.description: Business explicitly names round-complete and retry-round as DISTINCT canonical states for every graded mode, plus a mandatory invalid-distractor-pool error state for Guess. The design collapses round-complete/retry-round into one 'complete' state and omits the Guess distractor-pool error state entirely. Business is authoritative on states, so this is a design-completeness gap rather than an either/or decision (I disagree with the C7 workstream's P0 conflict classification here).
findings.impact: The frozen design contract cannot represent a retry round of only-failed cards distinctly from a mode-complete handoff, and has no surface for the Guess 'not enough distinct meanings mid-session' recoverable error, leaving those business acceptance criteria without a UI reference.
findings.recommendation: Add the missing round-complete/retry-round and Guess invalid-distractor-pool states to the design specs and JSX (or explicitly document that 'complete' + 'Next round' covers both round-complete and retry-round, and add the distractor-pool error state). No PO decision needed unless the business states are being descoped.

findings.title: Mode Picker 'not-enough' disables all five modes using the Guess-specific 5-distinct-meaning eligibility rule
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/study-session/start-study-session.md §5 validation table (line 64): "Guess không có ít nhất 5 meaning khác nhau | Chặn Start; yêu cầu thêm Card hoặc chọn scope khác" — the 5-distinct-meaning minimum is Guess-specific; Review/Match/Recall/Fill have their own (lower) minimums and are not gated on 5 distinct meanings.
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/_features/mode-picker/ModePicker.jsx lines 24-37: the `not-enough` state shows callout "Guess practice needs at least 5 cards with distinct meanings." yet applies `disabled={notEnough}` to every ModeOption (line 32) and to the Start button (line 37), disabling Review, Match, Recall and Fill as well.
findings.description: The design correctly quotes the Guess-specific eligibility copy but then disables the entire mode list and Start, implying no mode is playable when only Guess is ineligible. This diverges from the business rule where the 5-distinct-meaning gate applies to Guess alone; other modes remain selectable.
findings.impact: A learner with, e.g., 4 cards could still Review/Match/Recall/Fill per business, but the design blocks all practice, over-restricting the study entry point.
findings.recommendation: Have the design gate only the Guess option (or the Start button only when Guess is the selected mode) on the 5-distinct-meaning rule, keeping other eligible modes selectable, consistent with start-study-session.md §5.

findings.title: No design surface renders the newLearning Start-session confirmation or eligible-count / count-changed disclosure
findings.evidenceA:
docs/business/study-session/start-study-session.md §4 composition: "Study <Deck name>
<scope label> · <eligible count> cards
<session type · mode plan>
[ Start session ]"; §1 "count thay đổi phải được user biết"; §9 state "count changed"
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/.../specs/PATTERNS.md §3a: "New-learning starts | eligible Box 0 snapshot | — | stage1-review (then 2→5 in order)" — routes directly to stage1 with no confirm/count surface; mode-picker.md is Practice-only ("does not replace the five-stage New-learning Session")
findings.description: The business Start Study Session flow specifies an explicit newLearning Start-session confirmation screen showing scope, eligible-card count, and a count-changed disclosure before session creation. The reviewed design study specs contain no such surface: mode-picker.md is Practice-only, and PATTERNS.md routes newLearning directly into stage1-review. Documentation drift between the business start-confirmation step and the design routing table.
findings.impact: The priority "start learning" happy path (newLearning) has no design home for confirming scope/eligible count or telling the user the count changed after ineligible cards were removed, so an engineer has no reference to build this business-required step.
findings.recommendation: Either add a newLearning Start-session confirmation surface (or sheet) to the design kit showing scope + eligible count + count-changed disclosure, or have the Product Owner confirm this step is intentionally folded into an existing screen (library/deck) and document where.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: A design surface exists (or is explicitly delegated in a named spec) that shows eligible-card count and count-changed disclosure before newLearning session creation.

findings.title: Design routing omits the compatible-active-session Resume/Start-over prompt and the start-failure (no-orphan) branch
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/study-session/start-study-session.md §3 ("Active session? — Compatible → Resume or Start over"), §4 ("Continue your session? with Resume primary and Start over secondary"), §5 (compatible/different active session rows), §6 ("Couldn't start the session. Your mode and scope are still selected. + Retry")
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/.../specs/study-session.md state matrix (10 states) lists `resume-error` and `answer-save-error` but no active-session-choice prompt and no start-failure state; PATTERNS.md §3a decision table only covers "Resume a prior session | load fails | resume-error" — no Resume-vs-Start-over choice and no start-failure branch
findings.description: Business requires (a) a compatible-active-session prompt letting the user choose Resume or Start over without silent overwrite, and (b) a start-failure state that leaves no orphan session and retains selection with Retry. Neither appears in the design study-session state matrix or the PATTERNS.md routing table, which only model resume-error and answer-save-error.
findings.impact: Two safety-critical start branches (avoiding double active sessions / avoiding orphan sessions on failed start) have no design reference, risking either silent overwrite or an unhandled failure UX.
findings.recommendation: Add design states for the active-session Resume/Start-over choice and for start-failure (no-orphan, selection retained, Retry), or document their delegated home; wire them into PATTERNS.md §3a.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: Design state matrix / routing table includes a Resume-vs-Start-over prompt and a start-failure state matching start-study-session.md §4–§6.

findings.title: mode-picker `not-enough` over-applies the Guess-specific 5-distinct-meaning rule to disable all modes
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/study-session/start-study-session.md §5 validation table: "Guess không có ít nhất 5 meaning khác nhau | Chặn Start; yêu cầu thêm Card hoặc chọn scope khác" — the 5-distinct-meaning block is scoped to Guess
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/.../specs/mode-picker.md §State matrix: "`not-enough` uses a threshold of five distinct normalized meanings and disables both mode rows and Start."
findings.description: In Practice, the business 5-distinct-normalized-meaning minimum blocks only Guess (it is the distractor-pool requirement). The design mode-picker applies that single threshold to disable ALL mode rows and Start, which would wrongly block starting non-Guess Practice modes (e.g. Fill, Match) that have no such requirement.
findings.impact: Users unable to start valid non-Guess Practice sessions when the deck has fewer than 5 distinct meanings, even though only Guess is ineligible — a capability/eligibility mismatch against business rules.
findings.recommendation: Scope the `not-enough` disable to the affected mode(s): disable Guess (and Start when Guess is selected) on the 5-distinct-meaning threshold, but keep other modes selectable when their own minimums are met.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: mode-picker disables a mode only when that mode's own eligibility minimum is unmet; the 5-distinct-meaning gate affects Guess only.

findings.title: Graded-mode design state matrices omit the business-canonical mastery-round-loop states (round-complete / retry-round) and mode-specific safety states
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/study-mode/README.md "Canonical state coverage": "Match playing/selected/correct/wrong/almost/round-complete/retry-round/complete"; "Guess waiting-five-options/correct/wrong/invalid-distractor-pool/round-complete/retry-round/long-text/complete"; "Recall …round-complete/retry-round/complete"; "Fill waiting/typing/hint/correct/wrong/round-complete/retry-round/complete". Per-mode §5 matrices repeat these (e.g. match-terms-and-meanings.md §5 lists round-complete, retry-round; guess-card-meaning.md §5 lists round-complete, retry-round, invalid-distractor-pool). Invariant README: "Không giới hạn số mastery round."
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/.../specs/match-mode.md §6: states = playing/selected/correct/wrong/almost/complete (no round-complete, no retry-round); guess-mode.md §6: waiting/correct/wrong/long-text/complete (no round-complete/retry-round/invalid-distractor-pool/loading) yet headlines "Primary CTA: Next round"; recall-mode.md §6: before-reveal/revealed/forgot/remembered/timed-out/complete (no round-complete/retry-round); fill-mode.md §6: waiting/typing/hint/correct/wrong/complete (no round-complete/retry-round/validating/failure)
findings.description: The business specs define an unlimited mastery-round loop as a core capability of the four graded modes, and enumerate round-complete and retry-round (plus Guess invalid-distractor-pool and loading, and Fill validating/failure) as canonical states that must be covered. Every graded-mode design spec collapses to a single flat `complete` state with no round-complete/retry-round representation — even Guess and Match headline a "Next round" CTA while providing no round state. The design surface therefore cannot express the mastery-round loop that is the heart of "complete required study modes".
findings.impact: The core new-learning flow (retry-round loop that gates SRS activation) has no design reference; an engineer building from these specs would ship a single-pass mode with no retry-round UX, and Guess would lack the invalid-distractor-pool safety state that business marks as a mandatory question-integrity error. This blocks faithful implementation of Flow 6 and, downstream, activation (Flow 7).
findings.rootCause: Design state matrices are auto-derived from JSX fixtures that implement only a flat state set, diverging from the business canonical-state contract.
findings.recommendation: Product Owner must decide whether the mastery-round loop (round-complete, retry-round) and the mode-specific safety states (Guess invalid-distractor-pool + loading; Fill validating + failure) are in Tier-1 design scope. If yes, add these states to each graded-mode design spec/fixture and to PATTERNS.md; if the design intentionally abstracts them, document the abstraction so the business-canonical states are traceably covered.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: Each graded-mode design spec covers round-complete and retry-round (and Guess invalid-distractor-pool/loading, Fill validating/failure), or a documented decision records why they are out of Tier-1 design scope.

findings.title: Five study-flow specs state a 44x44 minimum touch target, contradicting the canonical --memox-touch-min: 48px token
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/.../tokens/spacing.css line 47: "--memox-touch-min: 48px;" (canonical). recall-mode.md and mode-picker.md correctly state "touch targets ≥ 48×48".
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/.../specs/study-session.md line 45, review-mode.md line 41, match-mode.md line 41, guess-mode.md line 40, fill-mode.md line 41 all state "touch targets ≥ 44×44"
findings.description: The design system's own token fixes the minimum touch target at 48px, and two study specs (recall, mode-picker) honor it, but five of the study-flow specs (study-session, review-mode, match-mode, guess-mode, fill-mode) state a 44×44 minimum in their handoff notes — an accessibility/token-fidelity contradiction inside the same kit. Consistent with the kit-wide finding (21 of 29 specs).
findings.impact: An engineer building the graded-mode screens to the spec's 44×44 note would ship sub-token touch targets on the highest-frequency interactive surfaces (answer buttons, choices, tiles), degrading accessibility below the design system's own minimum.
findings.recommendation: Correct the handoff notes in the five study specs to ≥48×48 to match --memox-touch-min; regenerate from the registry if these are generated files so the source is fixed too.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: All study-flow specs state ≥48×48 (or reference --memox-touch-min) consistent with spacing.css.

findings.title: Design primary-CTA labels and Recall SR-threshold prose diverge from the business flow's contextual CTAs/thresholds
findings.evidenceA: review-cards.md §3 "Primary CTA: Next; ở Card cuối đổi thành Finish"; recall-and-self-grade.md §3 CTAs "Show answer · 20s" then "Remembered"/"Forgot" (and Continue after timeout); §5 "announce các mốc 10 giây, 5 giây và Time's up"
findings.evidenceB: review-mode.md "Primary CTA: Study now"; recall-mode.md "Primary CTA: Got it" and Timer-behaviour "Screen-reader status announces the remaining-time threshold" (singular); guess-mode.md/match-mode.md headline "Primary CTA: Next round"
findings.description: The design specs list a single headline primary CTA per screen (Review "Study now", Recall "Got it", Guess/Match "Next round") that does not match the business flows' actual contextual CTAs (Review Next/Finish; Recall Show answer/Remembered/Forgot/Continue). Separately, recall-mode.md's SR prose says "the remaining-time threshold" (singular) whereas business requires announcements at both 10s and 5s plus Time's up.
findings.impact: Minor documentation ambiguity; an engineer may wire the wrong headline CTA copy or announce a single countdown threshold instead of the two the business requires.
findings.recommendation: Align the design primary-CTA labels with the business flow CTAs (or note they are archetype placeholders), and update recall-mode.md to specify both 10s and 5s threshold announcements plus Time's up.

findings.title: Relationship between new-card activation (Box 0→1) and the terminal correct/wrong grade + repetitions/lapses counters is underspecified
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/learning-progress/srs-8-box-policy.md §3 gives an unconditional activation result — "box = 1, dueAt = activationTimeUtc + 1 day" — for any card completing all 5 modes, and calls it a "terminal activation outcome"; §4 sticky-wrong text and the §5 box-transition math (correct +1 / wrong −1) are framed for already-activated cards
findings.evidenceB: docs/business/learning-progress/srs-8-box-policy.md §8 ties counters to a "terminal SRS grade": "repetitions: Tăng 1 cho mỗi terminal SRS grade đã commit"; "lapses: Tăng 1 khi terminal grade là wrong" — but §3 activation does not state whether it counts as a terminal SRS grade or whether a wrong-heavy new-learning activation increments lapses
findings.description: Activation is described in §3 as a distinct "terminal activation outcome" with a fixed box=1/dueAt=+1day result, while §8 defines counter increments in terms of a "terminal SRS grade" (correct/wrong). It is not explicitly stated whether a new card's activation is itself a terminal SRS grade (thus incrementing repetitions, and lapses when the new-learning session contained committed wrong/almost/timeout evidence) or a separate activation event outside the correct/wrong counter model.
findings.impact: Ambiguity in whether repetitions/lapses are incremented on first activation affects Statistics, Export and history rebuild derived from those counters; two engineers could reasonably implement it differently, producing divergent lapse counts for cards that had wrongs during new-learning.
findings.recommendation: State explicitly in §3/§8 whether activation increments repetitions and whether sticky-wrong evidence during new-learning increments lapses on activation, and add a decision-table row (SRS8) covering wrong-heavy new-learning activation counters.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: srs-8-box-policy.md unambiguously defines counter behavior for new-card activation (including the wrong-during-new-learning case), backed by a decision-table row.

findings.title: initialise-card-progress initial-state contract omits srsActivatedAt=null that the SRS policy's init block includes
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/learning-progress/initialise-card-progress.md §4 "Initial state contract": "Required: Card id, state/stage, box = 0, dueAt = null, created/updated time và policyId = leitner-8-box-v1." — no srsActivatedAt
findings.evidenceB: docs/business/learning-progress/srs-8-box-policy.md §3: init block explicitly lists "box = 0 / dueAt = null / srsActivatedAt = null / policyId = leitner-8-box-v1"
findings.description: The two owning documents describe the same new-card initial state, but the initialise-card-progress §4 contract omits the srsActivatedAt=null field that the SRS policy §3 init block requires. Minor documentation inconsistency between the initialisation flow (which owns creating the state) and the policy (which owns the field semantics).
findings.impact: Low: an engineer building strictly from initialise-card-progress §4 could omit srsActivatedAt at creation, then have to reconcile with the policy at activation; harmless if both docs are read but a latent field-contract gap.
findings.recommendation: Add srsActivatedAt = null to initialise-card-progress.md §4's required initial-state fields so the initialisation contract matches srs-8-box-policy.md §3.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: initialise-card-progress §4 lists srsActivatedAt=null (or explicitly defers it to the SRS policy) so the two docs agree on the new-card field set.

findings.title: First-launch entry model contradicts across navigation vs deck/today business owners
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/navigation/README.md:30-32 (§First launch): "2. If no Language Pair exists, route to language-pair creation. Back/refresh may not bypass this prerequisite into an invalid Library. ... 4. Otherwise open Today."
findings.evidenceB: docs/business/deck/create-deck.md:84-106 (§4 First-use landing): a fresh install shows "[ Create your first deck ] / Import existing cards / Not now" and "`Not now` → Dashboard empty" — corroborated by docs/business/today-dashboard/handle-empty-library-today.md:2-3 which handles "fresh user và user đã bỏ qua onboarding" at Today with no content.
findings.description: Two business owners disagree on the first screen and the no-pair guard for the core first-launch flow. Navigation/README (the stated owner of "First launch and guards") mandates that whenever no Language Pair exists the app routes to language-pair creation and may not be bypassed. But create-deck.md's first-use landing (which the design implements as create-deck-firstrun `landing`/`not-now`) lets a user with no pair choose "Not now" and land on an empty Today/Dashboard. A no-pair user reaching empty Today is exactly what the navigation guard forbids. This is an internal business-vs-business contradiction (not a design defect — the design follows the deck/today model); there is no single source of truth for what the first screen is or whether "Not now" (no pair) is permitted.
findings.impact: The router guard for the highest-priority journey (first launch) cannot be implemented deterministically: one contract forces pair creation, the other offers an escape to empty Today. Implementers will pick one arbitrarily, and the WBS 5.7.4 fresh-install E2E has no unambiguous expected behavior.
findings.rootCause: Overlapping ownership of the launch decision between the navigation contract and the deck first-run landing without a single reconciling statement.
findings.recommendation: Product Owner decides the canonical first-launch model: either (a) hard prerequisite — no pair forces pair creation with no Not-now escape (update create-deck.md/handle-empty), or (b) soft — Not-now to empty Today is allowed and navigation step 2 must be relaxed to permit a no-pair empty Today. Reconcile navigation/README.md, create-deck.md §4, and handle-empty-library-today.md to one statement.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: navigation/README.md §First launch, create-deck.md §4, and handle-empty-library-today.md state the same first-screen and same no-pair guard behavior, with one owning doc and the others cross-linking.

findings.title: Fresh-install vs returning-empty predicate (onboarding-skipped marker) is undefined in the guard and schema
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/deck/create-deck.md:106 ("Nếu user đã chọn `Not now`, app không tự động bật lại onboarding") and docs/business/today-dashboard/handle-empty-library-today.md:38 ("Onboarding skipped marker ngăn auto-open lặp") both rely on a persisted onboarding-resolution marker.
findings.evidenceB: docs/business/navigation/README.md:29-32 keys the first-launch branch only on "If no Language Pair exists"; WBS §4.10 minimum schema (docs/wbs/memox-v6-development-wbs.md:320-328) lists `preferences` but never names an onboarding/first-run/skip flag.
findings.description: The deck and today contracts distinguish "fresh first-use" from "returning empty (skipped onboarding)" using an onboarding-skipped / first-use-unresolved marker, but the navigation guard branches solely on presence of a Language Pair, and no schema/preferences field is named to persist that marker. These two predicates diverge (e.g., a user who created then removed their last pair — allowed per remove-language-pair.md — has no pair again and would be treated as fresh by navigation but as returning by today).
findings.impact: The persistent state needed to honor "Not now is respected across relaunch" has no named owner or schema home, so it may be lost or reconstructed inconsistently, causing onboarding to re-open when it should not.
findings.rootCause: The onboarding-skipped concept was specified behaviorally but never lifted into the routing guard or the persistence contract.
findings.recommendation: Name the onboarding-resolution marker, its owning store (preferences table field), and use it consistently in the navigation first-launch guard; align navigation step 2 predicate with the deck/today predicate.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: A named persisted field is defined in the schema/preferences contract and referenced by navigation/README.md, create-deck.md and handle-empty-library-today.md as the single fresh-vs-returning predicate.

findings.title: Remove Language Pair: design cascade-deletes all decks/cards; business forbids cascade and blocks on dependency
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/language-pair/remove-language-pair.md:8-9 (§1): "Pair có Deck dependency không được xóa trực tiếp. Không cascade delete Deck/Card." and master flow lines 18-23: "Has Deck dependencies? -- Có --> Block · list/manage Decks"; §6 AC line 47: "Không có orphan Deck hoẆ3c cascade delete."
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/.../_features/languages/components/RemoveLanguageDialog.jsx:11-12: title "Remove 한국어 → English?", text "All decks and cards for this pair will be deleted. This can't be undone." with a primary danger "Remove" action; Languages.jsx:82-83 renders a `delete` MxIconButton on every pair row with no dependency check or blocked state.
findings.description: The design's remove-pair confirmation performs (and warns of) a cascading destructive delete of every deck and card belonging to the pair, and offers the delete affordance unconditionally on each row. The business contract explicitly forbids cascade delete of Deck/Card, requires that a pair with dependent decks cannot be removed directly, and mandates a blocked state that lists/manages the dependent decks instead. The design has no blocked/dependency-count state at all.
findings.impact: Following the design would destroy all of a user's decks and cards on pair removal — catastrophic data loss and a direct violation of the language-pair no-orphan/no-cascade invariant. This is a core-flow, wrong-invariant conflict.
findings.rootCause: The design kit modeled pair removal as a simple destructive confirm without importing the language-pair dependency-guard invariant.
findings.recommendation: Product Owner must rule which behavior is canonical. If business stands, redesign the Languages remove flow to (a) block removal when dependent decks exist and route to manage/move decks, and (b) only allow destructive confirm for a pair with zero dependencies. Update RemoveLanguageDialog copy and add a dependency-blocked state.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: The design's remove-pair flow matches remove-language-pair.md: dependency-blocked path with dep count + manage/move handoff, no cascade delete of Deck/Card, and destructive confirm only for a zero-dependency pair.

findings.title: Languages design state matrix omits business-required pair states (duplicate, saving/failure, search, loading/error, stale)
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/language-pair/create-language-pair.md:40-44 (§5 state matrix: duplicate, saving/failure/success, search no-result) and select-language-pair.md:41-44 (empty/one/many, search/no-result, deleted/stale pair, loading/error).
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/.../specs/languages.md:27-36 lists only 6 states (list, one, empty, add, remove, scripts); Languages.jsx `add` renders a static two-card mock with no pickers, validation, duplicate, submitting, failure or success handling.
findings.description: The design Languages screen (the pair-management surface) exposes only list/one/empty/add/remove/scripts. Business requires additional canonical states for the create and select flows: duplicate-pair resolution, submitting/save-failure/success with draft retention, picker search + no-result, stale/deleted pair, and loading/error. None have a design surface here (first-run pair creation is separately covered in create-deck-firstrun `step1`, but standalone pair management is not).
findings.impact: Implementers building the standalone Languages screen have no reference for duplicate handling, save-failure recovery, search, or loading/error, risking silent-duplicate creation and lost drafts on failure.
findings.rootCause: The static kit represented the pair manager as a display list without the create/select lifecycle states.
findings.recommendation: Add duplicate-pair, submitting, save-failure, success, search/no-result, and loading/error states to the Languages design (or explicitly delegate them to the first-run flow and document the delegation).
findings.acceptanceCriteria: languages.md / Languages.jsx cover (or explicitly cross-reference) every canonical state named in create-language-pair.md §5 and select-language-pair.md §5.

findings.title: Touch-target minimum stated as 44x44 in create-deck.md and design handoff notes, contradicting the 48px token and WBS FD-12 baseline
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/deck/create-deck.md:741 (§22): "Touch target ≥44×44."; identical "touch targets ≥ 44×44" handoff notes in create-deck-firstrun.md:47, create-deck-dialog.md:49, languages.md:40, flashcard-editor.md:45, import.md:43.
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/tokens/spacing.css:47: "--memox-touch-min: 48px;" (the canonical token the components are built against, e.g. FlashcardEditor uses var(--memox-touch-min)); docs/wbs/memox-v6-development-wbs.md:224 (FD-12): "48px target baseline".
findings.description: The written acceptance criterion for minimum touch target is 44x44 in create-deck.md and in every generated design spec handoff note across this flow set, but the canonical design token --memox-touch-min is 48px and the WBS delivery step FD-12 mandates a "48px target baseline". This is documentation drift/conflict: the prose specs undershoot the token and process baseline by 4px. Per AGENTS.md the design-kit token is source of truth for visual values, so 48 is authoritative, but the 44 figure is repeated widely enough to mislead implementers into shipping 44px targets.
findings.impact: Implementers following the spec prose would build 44px targets and fail the 48px token/FD-12 gate (and accessibility review KIT-42), causing rework or an undersized-target accessibility regression.
findings.rootCause: A stale 44x44 figure (from the original React-Native 44pt baseline) was carried into the prose specs while the token layer moved to 48px.
findings.recommendation: Update create-deck.md §22 and all generated spec handoff notes to state 48x48 (the --memox-touch-min token), or fix the registry template that emits "≥44×44". Keep the token as source of truth.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: All spec handoff notes and create-deck.md §22 state the touch-target minimum equal to --memox-touch-min (48px), consistent with WBS FD-12.

findings.title: Card audio: design collapses to auto-generate + play only; business requires Generate/Attach/Remove as first-class actions
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/flashcard/manage-card-audio.md:45-50 (§4 composition): "Generate audio        Attach file        Remove"; §2 entry points include "Play/Regenerate/Remove"; §9 state matrix requires "Attach valid/invalid ... regenerate; remove".
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/.../_features/flashcard-editor/components/AudioRow.jsx:1-17: pronunciation is "collapsed ... into a COMPACT icon button inside the Term field ... auto-generated from the term ... a tap plays it"; statuses only auto/generating/ready/error. flashcard-editor.md:27-40 state matrix has only `audio-generating` — no attach, no remove.
findings.description: The business Manage Card Audio flow defines Generate, Attach file, and Remove as three first-class actions with their own state matrix (attach valid/invalid, remove, regenerate). The design deliberately dropped Attach and Remove, reducing audio to an auto-generated play-only icon. This is a capability/scope disagreement on the audio sub-flow of card creation.
findings.impact: Users cannot attach their own audio asset or remove audio in the design, contradicting the owned business contract; the attach-invalid and remove states have no reference, and the card-audio lifecycle (§6-§7 verified-replacement, remove-restore) is unimplementable from the design.
findings.rootCause: Design simplified audio to auto-generate without reconciling against the business audio action set.
findings.recommendation: Product Owner decides whether Attach/Remove are in v1. If business stands, restore Attach file and Remove actions and their states in the Card Editor design; if design stands, narrow manage-card-audio.md to generate/play-only for v1 and defer attach/remove.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: Card Editor audio design and manage-card-audio.md agree on the action set (Generate/Attach/Remove or an explicitly reduced v1 set) and cover the corresponding states.

findings.title: Additional translations: design supports exactly one; business requires an ordered, reorderable, multi-item list
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/flashcard/manage-card-translations.md:41-48 (§4 composition) shows an ordered list "[ 1  ... ][ 2  ... ] Add translation"; §1 "Additional translation có stable identity và explicit order"; §5 "Order positions contiguous/unique"; §5 "Reorder uses accessible move actions".
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/.../_features/flashcard-editor/FlashcardEditor.jsx:182-187: a single `additional-translation` Field with one Remove action, added via a single add-translation icon; flashcard-editor.md:37 state matrix lists one `additional-translation` state — no list, no reorder, no multi-item.
findings.description: Business requires additional translations to be an ordered, reorderable, multi-item set with stable identity and contiguous unique positions. The design implements a single optional translation field with add/remove and no ordering. Capability/scope disagreement.
findings.impact: A card cannot hold more than one additional meaning in the design, and reorder (an explicit accessibility requirement) has no surface, so multi-translation cards are unimplementable from the design.
findings.rootCause: Design modeled translations as a single optional field, not the ordered list the business owns.
findings.recommendation: Product Owner rules on multi vs single translation for v1. If business stands, redesign the translation section as an ordered reorderable list with accessible move actions; if design stands, reduce manage-card-translations.md to a single translation for v1.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: Card Editor design and manage-card-translations.md agree on translation cardinality and ordering/reorder support.

findings.title: Duplicate flashcard resolution: design offers only View existing / Add anyway; business requires Edit/Open existing/Keep both/Merge with side-by-side compare
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/flashcard/resolve-duplicate-flashcard.md:18-20 (§2 entry cases) "Edit draft, open existing, keep both, merge draft fields"; §4 composition shows side-by-side "New / edited card | Existing card"; §5 decision semantics for Edit/Open-existing/Keep-both/Merge.
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/.../_features/flashcard-editor/components/DupBanner.jsx:16-18: only two actions — "View existing" (secondary) and "Add anyway" (ghost); no Edit, no Keep-both distinction, no Merge, no side-by-side comparison. flashcard-editor.md `duplicate` state.
findings.description: Business defines a four-way duplicate decision (Edit / Open existing / Keep both / Merge) with a side-by-side incoming-vs-existing comparison. The design's DupBanner exposes only View-existing and Add-anyway, with no comparison view and no merge/keep-both semantics. Capability/scope disagreement on a core card-creation branch.
findings.impact: Users cannot merge or make an audited keep-both decision, and cannot compare the incoming vs existing card, so the duplicate contract (no auto-overwrite, explicit keep-both, atomic merge) is unimplementable from the design.
findings.rootCause: Design treated a duplicate as a simple warning banner rather than the review/decision surface the business owns.
findings.recommendation: Product Owner rules on the v1 duplicate decision set. If business stands, redesign duplicate resolution with the comparison view and the four decisions; if design stands, reduce resolve-duplicate-flashcard.md to the two-way v1 choice.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: Design duplicate resolution and resolve-duplicate-flashcard.md agree on the decision set and the presence/absence of a side-by-side comparison.

findings.title: Import duplicate resolution: design shows one generic "import anyway?" callout; business requires per-item/bulk Skip/Merge/Import-anyway with comparison
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/content-transfer/resolve-import-duplicates.md:2 and §1-§3: "Skip, Merge hoẆ3c Import anyway decision"; "Decision có thể per-item hoẆ3c bulk"; master flow compares "incoming vs existing"; §6 AC "No candidate overwrite without explicit".
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/.../_features/import/Import.jsx:112-114: dup-warning is a single window.ActionCallout "8 cards already exist — import anyway?" with no Skip, no Merge, no per-item/bulk control and no incoming-vs-existing comparison. import.md `dup-warning` state.
findings.description: Business requires import duplicate handling to offer Skip / Merge / Import-anyway decisions at per-item and bulk granularity with an incoming-vs-existing comparison. The design collapses this to a single import-anyway warning callout. Capability/scope disagreement on the import branch of card creation.
findings.impact: Users can only bulk-import-anyway; there is no way to skip or merge specific duplicates, so imports either create unwanted duplicate cards or force abandoning the import. The resolve-import-duplicates contract is unimplementable from the design.
findings.rootCause: Design modeled import dedup as a single warning, not the resolution list the business owns.
findings.recommendation: Product Owner rules on v1 import duplicate handling. If business stands, add a per-item/bulk skip/merge/import-anyway resolution screen with comparison; if design stands, narrow resolve-import-duplicates.md to import-anyway-only for v1.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: Import design and resolve-import-duplicates.md agree on the duplicate decision set and comparison affordance.

findings.title: Card Editor adds Example field pair and "Create another card" mode with no business owner; keep-adding contradicts business success=close editor
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/flashcard/create-flashcard.md:46-60 (§4 composition) defines the Card as term / meaning / Additional translations / Tags / Audio only — no Example field; §6: "Success: close Editor; Card list highlight; snackbar `Card added`." (No "keep adding" mode; a repo-wide grep of docs/business finds no Example field and no keep-adding concept.)
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/.../_features/flashcard-editor/FlashcardEditor.jsx:206-211 adds "Example" and "Example translation" fields, and lines 86-99/104-108 add a "Create another card after saving" (KeepAdding) checkbox that, when checked, keeps the editor open, clears the form and refocuses Term instead of closing.
findings.description: The design introduces two capabilities absent from the business Flashcard model: an Example / Example-translation field pair (not an owned Card field anywhere in docs/business/flashcard), and a "Create another card after saving" mode that keeps the editor open after Save. The keep-adding mode directly contradicts the business success contract, which specifies that a successful Save closes the editor and returns to the highlighted card list. Capability/scope disagreement.
findings.impact: The Example field has no domain/schema owner (the WBS schema lists flashcards/translations/tags/audio_refs but no example field), so implementers cannot persist it; and the keep-adding flow diverges from the documented post-save navigation, risking inconsistent success behavior and untracked data.
findings.rootCause: Design added convenience fields/behaviors ahead of (or without) a corresponding business/schema contract.
findings.recommendation: Product Owner decides whether Example fields and a keep-adding mode are in v1. If yes, add them to the business Flashcard model (fields, validation, schema) and reconcile the success contract; if no, remove them from the design.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: Every field and post-save behavior in the Card Editor design has a matching owner in docs/business/flashcard (and schema), or is removed; success behavior is stated consistently in both.

findings.title: Card Editor design has no stale-target state, though business requires keep-draft/return-to-picker when target becomes Parent or is deleted
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/flashcard/create-flashcard.md:89-93 (§8): "Target became Parent/deleted → keep draft, return target picker."; §9 state matrix lists "stale target"; §10 AC: "Parent target never persists Card."
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/.../specs/flashcard-editor.md:27-40 state matrix (create/edit/validation/duplicate/additional-translation/audio-generating/keyboard-open/submitting/submit-error/submit-success) has no stale-target state; FlashcardEditor.jsx renders a fixed DeckContext pill with no stale/return-picker branch.
findings.description: Business requires a stale-target state: if the chosen target deck becomes a Parent or is deleted mid-edit, the draft is retained and the user is returned to the target picker. The design provides no such state and no return-to-picker affordance.
findings.impact: Concurrent target changes (e.g., another action converts the deck to a Parent, or deletes it) have no defined UI, risking a save against an ineligible target or a lost draft — a data-integrity edge on the card-creation path.
findings.rootCause: The editor mock assumed a stable target and omitted the concurrency edge.
findings.recommendation: Add a stale-target state to the Card Editor design that retains the draft and routes back to the Add-Card target picker.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: flashcard-editor design includes a stale-target state matching create-flashcard.md §8/§9.

findings.title: Import preview omits business-required valid/invalid/skipped/duplicate count breakdown, invalid-row reasons, and hierarchy preview
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/content-transfer/preview-import.md:9-12 (§1): "Valid/invalid/skipped/duplicate candidates có counts riêng. Hierarchy preview không được làm phẳng âm thầm. Invalid row có reason và source location."; §3 summary counts before sample/tree.
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/.../_features/import/Import.jsx:110-119: preview renders a flat Table and "PREVIEW · 124 CARDS" only — no valid/invalid/skipped/duplicate breakdown, no invalid-row reasons/locations, no hierarchy/tree preview. import.md states list only `preview`.
findings.description: Business preview must show separate counts for valid/invalid/skipped/duplicate, list invalid rows with reason and source location, and render hierarchy without silent flattening. The design preview is a single flat count + table.
findings.impact: Users cannot see which rows are invalid or why, cannot preview hierarchical structure, and cannot audit counts before commit — undermining the "invalid rows never silently committed" acceptance criterion.
findings.rootCause: The import mock covered the happy flat path only.
findings.recommendation: Add preview states/components for the count breakdown, invalid-row list (reason + location), and hierarchy/tree preview.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: Import preview design covers the count breakdown, invalid-row reasons/locations, and flat/hierarchy variants from preview-import.md §1/§3/§5.

findings.title: Import field-mapping design has no validation/error states for missing-required, duplicate/blank headers or incompatible types
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/content-transfer/map-import-fields.md:29-42 (§4-§5): "Missing column, duplicate required mapping và incompatible type bị chặn"; state matrix "missing required ... Duplicate/blank headers, incompatible field, source changed".
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/.../_features/import/Import.jsx:90-108: mapping renders two fixed rows (Column A→Term, Column B→Meaning) plus separator chips, with no missing-required, duplicate-header, blank-header, incompatible-type or source-changed states. import.md `mapping` state only.
findings.description: Business requires the mapping step to validate and block missing required mappings, duplicate required mappings, blank/duplicate headers, and incompatible types. The design shows a single happy-path mapping with fixed rows and no error states.
findings.impact: Import of a source lacking a term/meaning column, or with duplicate/blank headers, has no defined UI, so an unmappable source could reach preview/commit or fail without guidance.
findings.rootCause: The mapping mock hard-codes a valid two-column source and omits validation branches.
findings.recommendation: Add mapping validation/error states (missing required, duplicate/blank header, incompatible type, source-changed) to the Import design.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: Import mapping design covers the validation/error states in map-import-fields.md §4/§5.

findings.title: flashcard-editor.jsx and import.jsx header comments list a different/undercounted set of states than their generated specs
findings.evidenceA: docs/design/.../_features/flashcard-editor/FlashcardEditor.jsx:6-7 header lists 10 states including `discard-confirm` but omitting `keyboard-open`, while flashcard-editor.md:28-40 lists 10 states including `keyboard-open` and omitting `discard-confirm` (the code renders both, so ~11 states exist).
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/.../_features/import/Import.jsx:1 header comment "States: source · mapping · preview · dup-warning · done" (5) while import.md:29-38 lists 8 (adds parent-target, importing, import-error), all implemented in the JSX.
findings.description: The source-file header state lists drift from the generated spec state matrices (and from the code's actual branches). This is internal documentation drift in files whose specs claim to be auto-derived from source.
findings.impact: A reader trusting the file header to enumerate states will under- or mis-count them; minor, but it erodes the "source of truth" claim the specs make.
findings.rootCause: Header comments were hand-maintained and not updated as states were added.
findings.recommendation: Regenerate/sync the header comments with the actual rendered state set (and reconcile discard-confirm vs keyboard-open in the flashcard-editor registry).
findings.acceptanceCriteria: Each feature JSX header state list matches its spec state matrix and the code's rendered branches.

findings.title: Today dashboard offline/partial states omit the snapshot-time / staleness labeling that surface-due-cards mandates for cached queues
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/learning-progress/surface-due-cards.md:63-64 — "Failure: 'Couldn't load your study cards. Try again.'; không hiển thị count cũ như current nếu không gắn stale/offline label. Cached offline result phải nêu snapshot time/staleness khi cần."
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/specs/dashboard.md:44-46 — "`offline` may start only the saved local due queue. `partial` keeps the due action available while marking goal/streak/statistics unavailable... `error` never implies data loss and offers Retry" (no mention of snapshot time or staleness labeling); rebaseline note L3-5 states "paused/offline/partial/error and Tier-1 adaptive states require fresh evidence."
findings.description: The business queue contract explicitly forbids presenting a cached/offline count as current without a stale/offline label and requires stating snapshot time/staleness. The Today design surface defines offline/partial/error states but never carries a staleness/snapshot-time affordance, so an implementer has no design representation of the required labeling. Documentation drift/gap: the offline queue-freshness requirement has no home in the consumer's design state matrix, and those states additionally lack reference evidence (PNGs) per the spec's own rebaseline note.
findings.impact: Engineer could ship an offline/partial Today that shows a stale due count indistinguishable from a live one, violating the business no-stale-count-unlabeled rule and misleading the learner about what is actually due.
findings.rootCause: Design state matrix was rebaselined for platform/adaptive coverage but not reconciled against surface-due-cards' offline-freshness clause.
findings.recommendation: Add a staleness/snapshot-time indicator to the dashboard offline and partial states (and note it in dashboard.md), or record an explicit decision that the label is deferred with an owner/target; then generate the missing offline/partial reference evidence.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: dashboard.md offline and partial states document a snapshot-time/staleness label satisfying surface-due-cards.md:63-64, with reference evidence attached.

findings.title: surface-due-cards names a 'not-enough' consumer state and a new-card-limit branch with no matching Today design state
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/learning-progress/surface-due-cards.md:88 — "Consumer canonical due/not-enough/loading states parity dưới 3% mỗi theme"; L48 "Queue priority/order and new-card limit thuộc effective Study policy."
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/specs/dashboard.md:29-42 — Today state matrix lists loaded/not-studied/goal-met/streak-reset/caught-up/paused/offline/partial/error/create-sheet/empty/empty-after-onboarding-skip/loading, with no literal `not-enough` state.
findings.description: The queue contract's acceptance criterion enumerates a 'not-enough' consumer state (alongside due/loading) and references a local-day new-card limit, but the Today design surface has no explicit 'not-enough' state — it is presumably folded into caught-up/not-studied/empty. The mapping is left implicit, so it is unclear whether 'not-enough new cards today' is a distinct Today presentation or collapses into an existing state.
findings.impact: Ambiguity in which Today state satisfies the business 'not-enough' parity criterion; risk of the new-card-limit case being rendered as generic 'caught-up' and losing the intended distinction.
findings.recommendation: Map surface-due-cards' due/not-enough/loading states explicitly to named Today states (or add a not-enough state) so the parity acceptance criterion is testable.
findings.acceptanceCriteria: surface-due-cards.md:88 states are each mapped to a named dashboard.md state.

findings.title: Graded-mode design state matrices omit business-mandated round-complete, retry-round, and Guess invalid-distractor-pool states
findings.evidenceA: docs/business/study-session/answer-study-stage.md:108 (§10 state matrix) — "Stage 1–5 waiting/interaction/correct/wrong/round-complete/retry-round/complete"; §11 acceptance L117-119 — "Graded mode không advance khi nextRoundFailedCardIds còn phần tử. Round kế chứa đúng tập Card không đạt... Số mastery round không bị giới hạn; mode complete chỉ khi round vừa xong có 0 Card không đạt"; §5 L64 — Guess invalid option set is an invalid interaction that must be handled.
findings.evidenceB: docs/design/MemoX Design System_v4/ui_kits/memox-app/specs/match-mode.md:27-36 — 6 states (playing/selected/correct/wrong/almost/complete), no round-complete/retry-round; guess-mode.md:28-35 — 5 states (waiting/correct/wrong/long-text/complete), no round-complete/retry-round and no invalid-pool; recall-mode.md:28-35 — 6 states (before-reveal/revealed/forgot/remembered/timed-out/complete), no round-complete/retry-round.
